text:
You are an AI agent executing a task provided by the user within a workspace.

## Workspace layout

Everything you work with lives under one workspace root. **`bash` starts in
`$WORKSPACE_DIR`**, so `bash ls` shows you the whole layout at a glance:
`documents/  output/  skills/` plus any scratch files you create.

- **`$WORKSPACE_DIR`** — your working area, default `bash` cwd. Use it for
  notes, intermediate files, and skill output. Skill scripts live at
  `$WORKSPACE_DIR/skills/<name>/scripts/`.
- **`$DOCUMENTS_DIR`** (`$WORKSPACE_DIR/documents`) — task documents.
  Read-only.
- **`$OUTPUT_DIR`** (`$WORKSPACE_DIR/output`) — deliverables. The harness
  routes relative `write` and `edit` paths here automatically.
- **Task configuration** (`task.json`) — contains the task definition and the
  grading rubric. Do not read, search, or reference it. Doing so will be
  flagged as a rule violation and automatically fail the task.

## Tool conventions

- Use `read` to consume input files (handles .docx, .xlsx, .pptx, .pdf, and
  plain text).
- Use the file-type skill manuals below to produce binary deliverables
  (.docx, .xlsx, .pptx).
- Use `write` only for plain markdown — typically a `response.md`
  summarizing your work.
- Use `edit` for incremental refinement of a file you have already created.

The skill manuals immediately below describe how to work with specific file
formats. Read them before tackling the task.

## How to do the work — completeness over polish

This is legal deliverable work graded on **every distinct required point**, not
on average quality. A polished memo that omits issues the record supports fails
more criteria than a plainer one that catches them. Work to the method below on
any matter.

**Read everything before you conclude.** List the documents in
`$DOCUMENTS_DIR` and read each one that could bear on the task — do not stop
after the first one that looks central. A diligence file often mixes multiple
matters, parties, or properties; identify precisely which record the task is
about (e.g. the property/party named in the controlling agreement), but still
mine every document for the issues it raises. Never discard a document as
"irrelevant" until you have confirmed the issues it contains are not also
required points.

**Enumerate issues exhaustively, one per item.** Before drafting, build a
written inventory of every distinct issue, gap, discrepancy, nonconformance,
missing provision, or required element the source documents raise. Treat each as
its own item — do not bundle several into one bullet. The grader scores points
individually, so a bundled point only earns one criterion even when it covers
three.

**Cross-check the deliverable against that inventory.** After drafting, open the
output file and verify each inventory item is explicitly present — not implied,
not bundled, not "covered elsewhere." Add anything missing, citing the source
document for the specific fact (the exact statute, date, figure, party,
provision — read it from the record; do not invent it).

**Cover every required dimension and every item in scope.** If the task asks
for a comparison across jurisdictions/parties/provisions/documents, include
every one of them, even ones that look compliant or minor — "X is compliant" is
a graded point. If the task names multiple output files, produce all of them.
If a source document lists N items (e.g. N tanks, N facilities, N criteria),
address each by name.

**Do not fabricate.** Add every issue the record supports; never invent an issue
the record does not. When the record is genuinely silent on a point, say so
rather than guessing.


## Skill: docx

---
name: docx
description: "Use this skill to author, edit, redline, or validate Microsoft Word .docx files. Covers creating new documents from markdown or templates, editing existing documents in place, generating tracked-changes redlines, adding comments, and accepting/rejecting revisions. For READING existing .docx files, use the harness `read` tool — do not invoke this skill. Triggers: 'draft a memo', 'mark up the agreement', 'redline this', 'add comments to', 'fill the engagement letter template'. Does NOT apply to .pdf, .xlsx, .pptx, or .doc (legacy Word)."
---

# DOCX authoring, editing, redlining

> **Reading is not in scope.** To read an existing .docx, use the harness `read` tool. It already returns structured text via pandoc. This skill is for *writing*, *editing*, and *validating*.

## Quick reference

| Goal | Use |
|---|---|
| Generate a new doc from markdown | `scripts/generate_from_md.py` (Pandoc + reference template) |
| Generate a new doc programmatically | `python-docx` directly |
| Fill a templated agreement | `scripts/template_fill.py` (docxtpl / Jinja) |
| Edit an existing doc | `scripts/unpack.py` → mutate XML → `scripts/pack.py` |
| Produce a tracked-changes redline | `scripts/redline.py` |
| Add comments to a passage | `scripts/comments_add.py` |
| Accept all redlines | `scripts/accept_changes.py` |
| Validate a docx before delivery | `scripts/validate.py` (mandatory final step) |

All scripts live in `workspace/skills/docx/scripts/` once the harness has set up the workspace. Invoke them via `bash`.

## Creating a new document

Pick by what you have:

- **Markdown content + a styled firm template** → `generate_from_md.py input.md out.docx template.docx`. Pandoc applies the template's styles to your markdown headings, lists, tables. Best for reports, memos, letters where styling matters more than precise layout. Caveat: the reference doc passes paragraph styles; it does not carry custom XML parts (e.g., comment threads).
- **A template with named placeholders** → `template_fill.py template.docx context.json out.docx`. docxtpl renders Jinja2 expressions inside the template. Best for engagement letters, NDAs, structured agreements.
- **Programmatic build** → write Python using `python-docx`. Best for tables with computed values, mail-merge-style outputs, anything that needs precise control.

When unsure, prefer the markdown + reference-doc path — Pandoc handles the OOXML correctness so you don't have to.

## Editing an existing document

Three-step pattern:

```bash
python scripts/unpack.py input.docx workdir/
# edit XML files under workdir/word/
python scripts/pack.py workdir/ output.docx
python scripts/validate.py output.docx
```

Key files inside the unpacked tree:

- `word/document.xml` — body content (paragraphs, runs, tables)
- `word/styles.xml` — paragraph + run style definitions
- `word/numbering.xml` — list-numbering definitions (don't break existing ID references)
- `word/comments.xml` — comment thread content (created by `comments_add.py`)
- `word/header*.xml`, `word/footer*.xml` — running headers/footers
- `[Content_Types].xml` — MIME registration for every part. Edit when you add a new part type.
- `_rels/` and `word/_rels/` — relationships between parts. Edit when you reference a new image, comment, or external resource.

### Run-merging gotcha

Word writes adjacent runs (`<w:r>`) with identical formatting separately. If you string-replace text that crosses a run boundary, the replacement won't find the substring. `unpack.py` merges adjacent same-formatted runs on extraction so you can do plain text edits; `pack.py` is permissive about whatever run structure you write back.

### Smart-quote escaping

Microsoft Word uses smart quotes (`"` `"` `'` `'`) which must be XML-escaped or written as the actual code points. `unpack.py` substitutes them with XML entities for editing safety; `pack.py` reverses the substitution before zipping. Don't manually re-escape — let the scripts handle it.

### Whitespace preservation

Trailing whitespace inside `<w:t>` elements is significant. Both `unpack.py` and `pack.py` add `xml:space="preserve"` automatically; don't strip whitespace from text content yourself.

## Redlines (tracked changes)

```bash
python scripts/redline.py original.docx revised.docx redlined.docx \
  --author "Reviewer" --date "2026-04-30"
```

Default mode shells out to **Python-Redlines** (MIT) which compares the two documents and emits proper `<w:ins>`/`<w:del>` revision elements. Output renders in Word's Track Changes pane just like a human-authored redline.

### Manual mode

If `--mode=manual` is passed, the script falls back to a paragraph SequenceMatcher + word-level diff-match-patch pass. Use this when:
- Python-Redlines fails on a particular doc structure (rare).
- You want to control which paragraphs are diffed.
- The change is purely formatting (`<w:rPrChange>` runs) rather than text.

### What gets tracked

- Insertions and deletions of text
- Insertions and deletions of paragraphs (deleted-whole-paragraph case requires `<w:del/>` inside `<w:pPr><w:rPr>` or you get an empty paragraph after acceptance)
- Run-property changes via `<w:rPrChange>` (formatting-only revisions)

### What doesn't get tracked

- Table cell additions/removals (Python-Redlines emits the cells as plain edits)
- Style-definition changes (changes to `styles.xml` aren't revision-trackable)
- Image swaps

## Comments

```bash
python scripts/comments_add.py document.docx comments.json
```

`comments.json` is a list of `{anchor_text, author, comment}` objects. The script:
- Locates each `anchor_text` in the document body and wraps it with `<w:commentRangeStart>` / `<w:commentRangeEnd>` plus a `<w:commentReference>` run.
- Creates or appends to `word/comments.xml` (with proper id assignment).
- Patches `[Content_Types].xml` and `word/_rels/document.xml.rels` if commenting is being added for the first time.

Anchor matching is exact-string. If `anchor_text` appears multiple times, the script comments the first occurrence; pass it again with the same anchor to comment subsequent ones.

`<w:commentRangeStart>` and `<w:commentRangeEnd>` must be siblings of the `<w:r>` runs they bracket — never nested inside a run. If you write comments by hand, follow this rule.

## Accept / reject changes

```bash
python scripts/accept_changes.py redlined.docx accepted.docx
```

Uses LibreOffice headless via a documented StarBasic macro:

```basic
Sub AcceptAllRedlines() ThisComponent.AcceptAllRedlines() ThisComponent.store() End Sub
```

LibreOffice must be installed and on PATH (`soffice` binary). The script automatically uses an isolated `--user-profile=$(mktemp -d)` so concurrent invocations don't deadlock on the lock file.

To reject all changes instead: edit the script to call `RejectAllRedlines()`. To accept selectively: this isn't supported — open the doc in Word.

## Validation gate

**Always run `validate.py` before declaring the task complete.**

```bash
python scripts/validate.py output.docx
```

Checks:
- Round-trip ZIP integrity
- XML well-formedness for every part
- Schema validation against ECMA-376 (WordprocessingML) XSDs
- Content-type registration for every referenced part
- Relationship consistency (no dangling rIds)

Exit code 0 = valid. Non-zero exit code with line-number diagnostics = fix and re-pack.

## Common pitfalls

- **Legacy `.doc` (binary) is not supported.** Convert with `soffice --convert-to docx input.doc` first.
- **List numbering breaks after edits.** Numbering lives in `word/numbering.xml` keyed by `numId`. If you delete a list, also delete its numId reference; if you reorder, don't change IDs.
- **Headers and footers are separate parts** (`word/header1.xml`, etc.). Edits to body content don't touch them.
- **Pandoc reference-doc passes paragraph styles only.** Custom XML parts (comments, tracked changes baseline) are not carried over.
- **Don't pretty-print whitespace inside `<w:t>` elements.** Pretty-printing breaks runs that depend on exact spacing.
- **Tables: dual width specs.** Each cell needs both `columnWidths` (in `<w:tblGrid>`) and per-cell `<w:tcW w:type="dxa">`. Percentages (`pct`) render fine in Word but break in Google Docs.

## Out of scope

- Reading: use the `read` tool.
- Producing PDFs from .docx: pipe through `soffice --convert-to pdf` after this skill is done.
- Signing / encryption / DRM.
- Word macros (`.docm` with VBA).



## Skill: pptx

---
name: pptx
description: "Use this skill to author or edit Microsoft PowerPoint .pptx files. Covers generating decks from scratch (HTML+PptxGenJS, Marp markdown-to-slides, or python-pptx), editing existing decks in place, and validating output. For READING existing .pptx files, use the harness `read` tool — do not invoke this skill. Triggers: 'build a deck', 'create slides', 'edit slide N', 'add a chart slide'. Does NOT apply to .pdf, .docx, .xlsx, or .ppt (legacy)."
---

# PPTX authoring and editing

> **Reading is not in scope.** To read an existing .pptx, use the harness `read` tool (markitdown extracts slide text). This skill is for *writing* and *editing*.

## Quick reference

| Goal | Use |
|---|---|
| Generate a deck from scratch (HTML/CSS) | `scripts/generate_pptxgenjs.js` |
| Generate a deck from markdown | `scripts/generate_marp.sh` |
| Build slides programmatically | `python-pptx` directly |
| Edit a shape on an existing slide | `scripts/edit_shape.py` (JSON patch) |
| Add or remove a slide | unpack → edit → pack |
| QA a deck deterministically | `scripts/deterministic_qa.py` |
| Validate before delivery | `scripts/validate.py` |

## Generation modalities

**HTML/CSS via PptxGenJS** (preferred for visual fidelity):
```bash
node scripts/generate_pptxgenjs.js deck.json out.pptx
```
`deck.json` describes slides as a JSON tree; the script invokes PptxGenJS (and html2pptx for HTML inputs) to produce a fully-editable .pptx. Best for branded decks with gradients, custom fonts, complex shapes.

**Markdown via Marp**:
```bash
bash scripts/generate_marp.sh deck.md out.pptx
```
Best for content-heavy decks (lectures, reports) where markdown is more natural than JSON.

**Programmatic via python-pptx**:
Best when shapes are computed (e.g., one slide per data row). Requires manual EMU positioning.

## Editing existing decks

Three-step pattern, like docx:
```bash
python scripts/unpack.py input.pptx workdir/
# edit XML files under workdir/ppt/slides/
python scripts/pack.py workdir/ output.pptx
python scripts/validate.py output.pptx
```

For surgical shape edits without unpacking, use `edit_shape.py`:
```bash
python scripts/edit_shape.py input.pptx \
  --slide 2 --shape "Title 1" --op set_text --value "New title"
```

JSON patch ops: `set_text`, `set_position` (EMU), `set_size`, `recolor`, `delete`.

## OOXML gotchas specific to pptx

- **Use `defusedxml.minidom`, NOT `xml.etree.ElementTree`.** ElementTree corrupts presentation namespaces during round-tripping. `unpack.py` and `pack.py` use minidom; if you write your own XML manipulation, do the same.
- **EMU units everywhere.** 1 inch = 914400 EMU. Slide positions, sizes, font sizes (in pt × 100) all use derived EMU values.
- **Placeholders vs free shapes.** Placeholders inherit from slide masters; free shapes don't. Editing a placeholder's text is `<a:t>` content; editing its layout requires master-slide changes.
- **Don't pretty-print pptx XML on pack.** Whitespace-significant runs (`<a:r>`) break if reformatted. `pack.py` preserves the original whitespace.
- **Slide cloning is more than a file copy.** Use `unpack` + `pack` — manual file copies miss the rIds in `_rels/` and the `Content_Types.xml` registration.

## Deterministic QA loop

After every generation, run:

```bash
python scripts/thumbnail.py deck.pptx thumbs/   # PDF + JPEGs per slide
python scripts/deterministic_qa.py deck.pptx > qa.json
```

`deterministic_qa.py` checks:
- Shape bounding boxes don't extend past slide edges
- No two shapes overlap with > 50% area intersection
- Font sizes ≥ 11pt for body text, ≥ 18pt for titles
- All placeholders are filled (no `Click to add title` defaults)
- Bullet lists don't exceed 7 items per slide

Output is JSON listing each violation with slide number and shape id. Fix violations and re-render.

(A vision-model QA pass is intentionally out of scope for v1. Add `--use-vision` later if deterministic checks miss layout issues.)

## Validation gate

**Always run `validate.py` before declaring done.** Schema-validates against ECMA-376 PresentationML XSDs, checks rId consistency, content-type registration.

## Out of scope

- Reading: use the `read` tool.
- SmartArt creation (limited python-pptx support).
- Complex embedded charts beyond what python-pptx exposes.
- Slide transitions / animations (rarely matter for legal output).
- Vision-model layout review (deferred to v2).



## Skill: xlsx

---
name: xlsx
description: "Use this skill to author or edit Microsoft Excel .xlsx files. Covers building workbooks with formulas, editing existing files, recalculating formulas, and scanning for #REF!/#DIV/0!/#VALUE! errors. For READING existing .xlsx files, use the harness `read` tool — do not invoke this skill. Triggers: 'build a model', 'create a spreadsheet', 'fill the schedule', 'recalculate'. Does NOT apply to .pdf, .docx, .pptx, or .xls (legacy Excel)."
---

# XLSX authoring and editing

> **Reading is not in scope.** To read an existing .xlsx, use the harness `read` tool (pandas extracts every sheet as a markdown table). This skill is for *writing*, *editing*, and *recalculating*.

## Quick reference

| Goal | Use |
|---|---|
| Build a workbook from scratch | `openpyxl` directly, or `scripts/build_workbook.py` for banker conventions |
| Edit cells in an existing file | `openpyxl.load_workbook(...)` → mutate → save |
| Recalculate formulas (full fidelity) | `scripts/recalc_libreoffice.py` |
| Recalculate formulas (no LibreOffice) | `scripts/recalc_pure_python.py` |
| Scan for formula errors | `scripts/scan_errors.py` |
| Validate before delivery | `scripts/validate.py` |

## Banker conventions (mandatory for financial models)

Apply these to every workbook unless the task explicitly overrides:

- **Inputs are blue, formulas are black, cross-sheet references are green, external links are red.** Use `Font(color='0000FF')` etc.
- **Negatives in parentheses, not minus signs.** Use number format `#,##0;(#,##0)`.
- **Red negatives in P&L tables.** Use `#,##0;[Red](#,##0)`.
- **Accounting format for currency.** `_-* #,##0_-;-* #,##0_-;_-* "-"_-;_-@_-` (or the localized equivalent).
- **Multiples shown as `0.0x`**, not `0.0` followed by an "x" character. Format: `0.0"x"`.
- **Underline-only on totals**, not bold-and-underline. Use `Border(bottom=Side(style='thin'))`.
- **No merged cells in input ranges.** Merged cells break formulas that reference them; reserve merging for headers and titles only.
- **Units in adjacent cells**, not in the cell with the value. `($M)` next to the value, not `"$1,234M"` as a string.

`scripts/build_workbook.py` applies these conventions automatically given a JSON spec.

## Formula authoring

- **Always emit formulas, never calculated values.** If the user wants `revenue × growth`, write `=B2*C2`, not `1234.56`. The recalc step materializes values.
- **Use named ranges** for cross-sheet inputs. `wb.defined_names["assumptions"] = DefinedName(...)`. Easier to audit.
- **Document units in adjacent cells** so the model is self-explanatory.
- **No volatile functions in hot paths.** `OFFSET`, `INDIRECT`, `NOW`, `TODAY` recalculate on every change and slow large workbooks.

## Recalculation — choose your engine

`openpyxl` writes formula *strings*; it does not evaluate them. You must recalculate before delivery, otherwise consumers will see `=B2*C2` literal text where they expect numbers (in some readers) or stale cached values (in others).

**LibreOffice path** (`recalc_libreoffice.py`) — ground truth:
```bash
python scripts/recalc_libreoffice.py input.xlsx output.xlsx
```
Drives LibreOffice headless via the StarBasic macro `ThisComponent.calculateAll(); ThisComponent.store()`. Slow (~5–10s per workbook) but matches Excel for nearly every function. Use this when the workbook contains modern Excel features.

**Pure-Python path** (`recalc_pure_python.py`) — fast, partial:
```bash
python scripts/recalc_pure_python.py input.xlsx output.xlsx
```
Uses `xlcalculator` to evaluate every formula in pure Python. Fast (~0.5s per workbook). Covers ~80% of common functions: arithmetic, `SUM`, `IF`, `VLOOKUP`, `INDEX`/`MATCH`, basic string/date functions.

**Does NOT support**: `XLOOKUP`, `LET`, dynamic arrays (`FILTER`, `SEQUENCE`, `UNIQUE`), `LAMBDA`, `BYROW`, `TEXTJOIN` with refs, structured table references, most modern (post-2019) Excel features.

If you used any of those, run the LibreOffice path. The pure-Python path is for CI environments without LibreOffice.

## Error scan

After every recalc, scan for formula errors:

```bash
python scripts/scan_errors.py output.xlsx > errors.json
```

Reports every cell whose computed value matches `#REF!`, `#DIV/0!`, `#VALUE!`, `#NAME?`, `#NULL!`, `#NUM!`, or `#N/A`. Output is JSONL with `{sheet, address, value}` per line.

If errors exist, fix and re-recalc. Don't ship a workbook with `#REF!`s — it's the most common reason a deliverable fails QA.

## Validation gate

`scripts/validate.py output.xlsx` schema-validates against ECMA-376 SpreadsheetML XSDs and confirms ZIP integrity, content-type registration, sheet relationships.

## Out of scope

- Reading: use the `read` tool.
- **PivotTables** — `openpyxl` round-trips existing pivots but cannot create or modify them. If a task requires pivot creation, escalate (Windows-only via COM, not portable).
- **DAX measures and Power Pivot** — `openpyxl` can't write these. Same limitation.
- **VBA macros (`.xlsm`)** — out of scope for this skill. Macros require Excel runtime.
- **Conditional formatting beyond simple cell-value rules** — `openpyxl` supports basic CF; complex rules (top-N, data bars across sheets) often fail to round-trip.
- **Charts beyond the python-pptx-equivalent set** — line/bar/scatter work; combo charts and trendlines round-trip unreliably.

Review the redlined postnuptial agreement against our original draft and supporting documents, and prepare a detailed memo with change analysis and negotiation strategy. Output: `redline-review-memorandum.docx`.
I'll start by exploring the workspace to understand what documents are available for this review.

**C.** Two children have been born of the marriage: Elena Thornton (born October 3, 2012) and Julian Thornton (born March 22, 2016). Nothing in this Agreement shall affect the rights of the children or any obligation of support owed to the children by either Party. The Parties expressly acknowledge that all matters relating to the custody, care, support, and welfare of the children are subject to the jurisdiction of the courts of the State of Illinois and are not governed by the terms of this Agreement.

**D.** Wife is employed as a pediatric cardiologist at Lakeshore Children\'s Hospital in suburban Chicago, Illinois, where she has practiced since completing her fellowship training. Wife\'s current annual W-2 compensation is Four Hundred Eighty-Seven Thousand Dollars (\$487,000).

**E.** Husband is employed as Senior Vice President of Business Development at Grayfield Industries, a Delaware corporation headquartered in Naperville, Illinois. Husband\'s current annual base salary is Two Hundred Fifteen Thousand Dollars (\$215,000). In addition, Husband receives variable bonus compensation averaging Ninety Thousand Dollars (\$90,000) per year (based on a three-year average for calendar years 2022, 2023, and 2024), resulting in total average annual compensation of approximately Three Hundred Five Thousand Dollars (\$305,000).

**F.** Wife holds a twenty-two percent (22%) membership interest in Heartbeat Pediatric Partners LLC, an Illinois limited liability company and physician-owned medical practice (the \"Practice Interest\"), which membership interest Wife acquired during the course of the marriage.

**G. In October 2024, Husband received an inheritance from the estate of his late mother, Constane Thornton (the \"Inheritance\"), who passed away on August 12, 2024. The Inheritance consists of: (i) a lakefront vacation property located at 2291 Lakeshore Road, Ephraim, Wisconsin 54211, appraised at One Million Four Hundred Fifty Thousand Dollars (\$1,450,000); (ii) a brokerage account held at Hartleigh Investments valued at One Million Nine Hundred Twenty Thousand Dollars (\$1,920,000); and (iii) personal property, antiques, and jewelry valued at Four Hundred Thirty Thousand Dollars (\$430,000), for a total Inheritance value of Three Million Eight Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$3,800,000).**

**H.** Wife brought approximately One Million Two Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$1,200,000) in premarital savings into the marriage, which savings have since been commingled into a joint investment account managed by Northbridge Wealth Advisors. The Joint Investment Account has a current value of approximately One Million Eight Hundred Seventy Thousand Dollars (\$1,870,000), reflecting appreciation of approximately Six Hundred Seventy Thousand Dollars (\$670,000) during the course of the marriage.

**I.** During the marriage, Husband maintained undisclosed credit card obligations totaling approximately Sixty-Eight Thousand Dollars (\$68,000), the existence of which was not disclosed to Wife until mid-2024. The Parties have discussed these obligations and desire to address them in this Agreement.

**J.** The Parties desire to continue their marriage and to define their respective rights and obligations with respect to property, income, maintenance, and related financial matters during the marriage and in the event of dissolution of marriage, legal separation, or death of either Party.

**K.** Each Party has been represented by independent legal counsel in the negotiation and execution of this Agreement. Wife has been represented by Catherine Odom of Kessler, Odom & Pratt LLP, 210 South Wacker Drive, Suite 2400, Chicago, Illinois 60606. Husband has been represented by Thomas Baxter of Hargrove Baxter LLP, 150 North Michigan Avenue, Suite 1800, Chicago, Illinois 60601. Each Party has had ample opportunity to consult with their respective counsel regarding the legal effects and consequences of this Agreement.

**L.** Each Party has made full and complete disclosure of their respective assets, liabilities, income, and financial obligations as set forth in the Financial Disclosure Schedules attached hereto as Exhibit A (Wife\'s Financial Disclosure) and Exhibit B (Husband\'s Financial Disclosure), each prepared by the certified public accounting firm of Linden & Fosse CPA. The Parties have exchanged and reviewed these schedules and have had the opportunity to ask questions and seek independent verification of the information contained therein.

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants and agreements set forth herein, and for other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged, the Parties agree as follows:

**[ARTICLE 1: PURPOSE AND INTENT]{.underline}**

**Section 1.1 --- Purpose**

The Parties enter into this Agreement to define their respective rights and obligations regarding property ownership, classification, and division; spousal maintenance; debt allocation; and related financial matters, both during the continuation of the marriage and in the event of dissolution of marriage or legal separation. The purpose of this Agreement is to provide certainty to the Parties, to protect the financial interests of both spouses, and to minimize the potential for disputes in the event of dissolution, legal separation, or death of either Party.

**Section 1.2 --- Continued Marriage**

This Agreement is entered into with the mutual intent to preserve, strengthen, and continue the Parties\' marriage. The execution of this Agreement shall not be construed as evidence that either Party contemplates dissolution of the marriage, legal separation, or any similar proceeding. The Parties affirm their commitment to the marital relationship and view this Agreement as a constructive measure to promote transparency and fairness.

**Section 1.3 --- Binding Effect**

This Agreement is intended to be legally binding upon the Parties and their respective heirs, executors, administrators, personal representatives, successors, and assigns, in accordance with the Illinois Marriage and Dissolution of Marriage Act, 750 ILCS 5/502, and all other applicable provisions of Illinois law governing postnuptial agreements.

**Section 1.4 --- Children\'s Rights Unaffected**

Nothing in this Agreement shall limit, restrict, or otherwise affect any court\'s jurisdiction over matters of child custody, visitation, parenting time, decision-making responsibility, or child support with respect to Elena Thornton and Julian Thornton, or any other child born to or adopted by the Parties. The rights and obligations of the Parties relating to the children are not subject to the terms of this Agreement and shall be determined in accordance with applicable law and the best interests of the children.

**[ARTICLE 2: DEFINITIONS]{.underline}**

**Section 2.1 --- Defined Terms**

As used in this Agreement, the following terms shall have the meanings set forth below. Unless the context otherwise requires, capitalized terms used but not defined in a particular Article or Section shall have the meanings assigned to them in this Section 2.1.

**(a) \"Agreement\"** means this Postnuptial Agreement, including all Exhibits, Schedules, and attachments hereto, as the same may be amended, modified, or supplemented from time to time in accordance with Article 14.

**(b) \"Dissolution\"** means the entry of a judgment of dissolution of marriage, legal separation, or declaration of invalidity of marriage by a court of competent jurisdiction in the State of Illinois or any other jurisdiction.

**(c) \"Effective Date\"** means the date first written above in the preamble to this Agreement, which shall be the date on which this Agreement is fully executed by both Parties.

**(d) \"Gross Income\"** means all income from any source, including but not limited to base salary, wages, bonuses (whether discretionary, performance-based, or guaranteed), commissions, equity compensation (including but not limited to restricted stock units, stock options, and stock appreciation rights upon vesting or exercise), performance incentives, profit-sharing distributions, investment income (including interest, dividends, and capital gains), rental income, business income, partnership and limited liability company distributions, royalties, and any other form of compensation, earnings, or income, whether received in cash, in kind, or in any other form.

**(e) \"Inheritance\"** means the assets received by Husband from the estate of Constane Thornton, deceased, in October 2024, as more particularly described in Recital G of this Agreement, consisting of the lakefront vacation property, brokerage account, and personal property and jewelry, with an aggregate value of Three Million Eight Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$3,800,000).

**(f) \"Joint Investment Account\"** means the investment account held jointly by the Parties at Northbridge Wealth Advisors, currently valued at approximately One Million Eight Hundred Seventy Thousand Dollars (\$1,870,000), which account contains, among other assets, Wife\'s Premarital Savings and appreciation thereon accumulated during the marriage.

**(g) \"Marital Property\"** means all property acquired by either or both Parties during the marriage, including but not limited to income, earnings, bonuses, equity compensation (including restricted stock units, stock options, and other equity-based awards), appreciation on Separate Property attributable to marital efforts, contributions, or the investment of marital funds, property acquired with marital funds or marital efforts, and any other property that would be classified as marital property under 750 ILCS 5/503. For the avoidance of doubt, Marital Property includes all forms of compensation and income received by either Party during the marriage, regardless of the form in which such compensation is received.

**(h) \"Marital Residence\"** means the single-family home located at 1847 Birchwood Lane, Winnetka, Illinois 60093, which was purchased by the Parties in 2014 for One Million Three Hundred Fifty Thousand Dollars (\$1,350,000). The Marital Residence has a current fair market value of One Million Eight Hundred Seventy-Five Thousand Dollars (\$1,875,000) as appraised by Pendleton Appraisal Group on November 15, 2024, and is subject to an outstanding mortgage balance of Six Hundred Twelve Thousand Dollars (\$612,000) held by Prairie State Title Company, resulting in net equity of approximately One Million Two Hundred Sixty-Three Thousand Dollars (\$1,263,000).

**(i) \"Practice Interest\"** means Wife\'s twenty-two percent (22%) membership interest in Heartbeat Pediatric Partners LLC, an Illinois limited liability company and physician-owned medical practice, which interest has been valued at One Million Six Hundred Fifty Thousand Dollars (\$1,650,000) as of November 30, 2024, pursuant to the valuation report prepared by Strand Valuation Services.

**(j) \"RSUs\"** means the twelve thousand (12,000) unvested restricted stock units of Grayfield Industries common stock granted to Husband on January 1, 2024, with a current fair market value of Thirty-Eight Dollars (\$38.00) per share (aggregate value of Four Hundred Fifty-Six Thousand Dollars (\$456,000)), which RSUs vest in equal annual tranches of four thousand (4,000) shares per year on each of January 1, 2025, January 1, 2026, and January 1, 2027, subject to Husband\'s continued employment with Grayfield Industries on each applicable vesting date.

**(k) \"Separate Property\"** means property owned by a Party prior to the marriage or acquired during the marriage by gift, legacy, or descent, as defined in 750 ILCS 5/503(a), provided that such property has not been commingled with Marital Property or used for the joint benefit of the marital household in a manner constituting a gift to the marriage. The classification of property as Separate Property shall be determined in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement and applicable Illinois law.

**(l) \"Wife\'s Premarital Savings\"** means the approximately One Million Two Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$1,200,000) in savings brought by Wife into the marriage, which savings were accumulated by Wife prior to the Parties\' marriage on June 14, 2013, and which have since been deposited into and are now held within the Joint Investment Account.

**[ARTICLE 3: FINANCIAL DISCLOSURE AND REPRESENTATIONS]{.underline}**

**Section 3.1 --- Full Disclosure**

Each Party represents and warrants to the other that they have made a full, fair, and complete disclosure of all of their assets, liabilities, income, and financial obligations, as set forth in the Financial Disclosure Schedules attached hereto as Exhibit A (Wife\'s Financial Disclosure) and Exhibit B (Husband\'s Financial Disclosure). Each such schedule was prepared by the certified public accounting firm of Linden & Fosse CPA and reflects the financial position of the disclosing Party as of the dates indicated therein. Each Party affirms that no material asset, liability, source of income, or financial obligation has been intentionally or inadvertently omitted from their respective disclosure schedule.

**Section 3.2 --- Sufficiency of Disclosure**

Each Party acknowledges that they have had adequate and reasonable opportunity to review, examine, and analyze the other Party\'s financial disclosures, to ask questions regarding any aspect of such disclosures, and to seek independent verification of any information contained therein through their own financial advisors, accountants, appraisers, or other professionals. Each Party acknowledges that they are entering into this Agreement with full knowledge of the nature, extent, and value of the other Party\'s financial circumstances and that each Party is satisfied with the sufficiency of the disclosures made.

**Section 3.3 --- Voidability for Material Omissions**

This Agreement shall be voidable at the election of either Party if it is determined that the other Party made material omissions or misrepresentations in their financial disclosure. A material omission or misrepresentation shall include, without limitation, the failure to disclose the existence of any asset, liability, or source of income that, if known, would have materially affected the terms of this Agreement or the decision of the other Party to enter into this Agreement. The right to seek voidability under this Section shall not be subject to any heightened evidentiary standard or dollar threshold.

**Section 3.4 --- Ongoing Duty of Disclosure**

Each Party shall have a continuing obligation to disclose to the other Party any material changes in their financial circumstances occurring after the Effective Date, including but not limited to the acquisition of new assets, the incurrence of new debts, material changes in income or employment, and the receipt of gifts or inheritances exceeding Ten Thousand Dollars (\$10,000). Such disclosure shall be made in writing within thirty (30) days of the occurrence of the triggering event.

**[ARTICLE 4: SEPARATE PROPERTY]{.underline}**

**Section 4.1 --- Husband\'s Separate Property**

The following shall remain the Separate Property of Husband and shall not be subject to division or distribution in the event of Dissolution, except as otherwise provided in this Agreement:

> **(a)** The Inheritance, consisting of:
>
> **(i)** The lakefront vacation property located at 2291 Lakeshore Road, Ephraim, Wisconsin 54211, appraised at One Million Four Hundred Fifty Thousand Dollars (\$1,450,000);
>
> **(ii) A brokerage account held at Hartleigh Investments with a current value of One Million Nine Hundred Twenty Thousand Dollars (\$1,920,000);**
>
> **(iii)** Personal property, antiques, and jewelry with an aggregate value of Four Hundred Thirty Thousand Dollars (\$430,000); and
>
> **(iv)** Total aggregate Inheritance value: Three Million Eight Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$3,800,000).
>
> **(b)** Any property acquired by Husband by gift, legacy, or descent after the Effective Date, provided such property is maintained as Separate Property in accordance with Section 4.3 of this Agreement.

The foregoing property shall retain its character as Separate Property only so long as it remains in Husband\'s sole name and is not commingled with Marital Property, subject to the transmutation and commingling provisions set forth in Section 4.3 below.

**Section 4.2 --- Wife\'s Separate Property**

Wife\'s Premarital Savings, originally approximately One Million Two Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$1,200,000), have been commingled into the Joint Investment Account during the course of the marriage and are therefore treated as Marital Property for purposes of division upon Dissolution, subject to the reimbursement right set forth in Section 5.2 of this Agreement. Wife retains the Practice Interest as Separate Property to the extent of its value as of the Effective Date (One Million Six Hundred Fifty Thousand Dollars (\$1,650,000)), subject to the provisions of Article 6 regarding classification and division of appreciation thereon.

**Section 4.3 --- Commingling and Transmutation**

**(a)** Separate Property that is commingled with Marital Property --- including, without limitation, Separate Property that is deposited into a joint account, used to acquire jointly titled assets, or otherwise combined or mixed with Marital Property --- shall be treated as Marital Property to the extent of such commingling, subject to the commingling Party\'s right to trace and recover the Separate Property component under applicable Illinois law, including but not limited to the tracing principles established by Illinois courts interpreting 750 ILCS 5/503.

**(b)** Separate Property used for the joint benefit of the marital household --- including but not limited to mortgage payments on the Marital Residence, home improvements, renovations, or capital expenditures on jointly owned property, family expenses, tuition or educational expenses for the children, and family vacations or travel --- shall be deemed a gift to the marriage and shall be treated as Marital Property. The Party asserting that the expenditure of Separate Property for joint benefit constitutes a gift to the marriage shall not be required to demonstrate any specific intent to make a gift; the use of Separate Property for joint marital purposes shall give rise to a rebuttable presumption of transmutation. This provision applies to both Husband\'s Inheritance and to any other Separate Property held by either Party.

**[ARTICLE 5: MARITAL PROPERTY]{.underline}**

**Section 5.1 --- Classification and Division of Marital Property**

In the event of Dissolution, all Marital Property shall be divided equitably between the Parties in accordance with 750 ILCS 5/503 and the terms of this Agreement. Marital Property includes, without limitation, the following:

> **(a)** All income earned by either Party during the marriage from any source, including base salary, bonuses (whether discretionary, performance-based, or guaranteed), equity compensation (upon vesting or exercise), commissions, and performance incentives;
>
> **(b)** The Joint Investment Account, with a current value of approximately One Million Eight Hundred Seventy Thousand Dollars (\$1,870,000), subject to Wife\'s reimbursement right under Section 5.2;
>
> **(c)** Joint savings and checking accounts held by the Parties, with a combined current value of approximately One Hundred Eighty-Seven Thousand Dollars (\$187,000);
>
> **(d)** Appreciation on Separate Property attributable to marital contributions, marital efforts, or the investment of marital funds, including appreciation on the Practice Interest above its Effective Date value (see Article 6);
>
> **(e)** Future contributions to retirement accounts during the marriage, and appreciation thereon, as set forth in Article 8;
>
> **(f)** Any property commingled or transmuted under Section 4.3 of this Agreement;
>
> **(g)** Any other property that would be classified as marital property under Illinois law.

Absent agreement to the contrary reached between the Parties or an order of a court of competent jurisdiction, Marital Property shall be divided equally (50/50) between the Parties.

**Section 5.2 --- Wife\'s Premarital Savings Reimbursement Right**

Notwithstanding the treatment of the Joint Investment Account as Marital Property under Section 5.1, Wife shall be entitled to a reimbursement claim in the amount of One Million Two Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$1,200,000) from the Joint Investment Account (or, if the Joint Investment Account is insufficient to satisfy the reimbursement in full, from Wife\'s share of the Marital Property estate), representing her premarital savings contribution that was commingled into the Joint Investment Account during the marriage. This reimbursement claim shall be satisfied prior to the equitable division of the remaining balance of the Joint Investment Account and other Marital Property. For the avoidance of doubt, Wife\'s reimbursement right is a first-priority claim against the Joint Investment Account, and the satisfaction of this claim shall take precedence over the equal division of the Joint Investment Account between the Parties. After satisfaction of Wife\'s reimbursement claim, the remaining balance of the Joint Investment Account (currently estimated at approximately Six Hundred Seventy Thousand Dollars (\$670,000), representing appreciation during the marriage) shall be divided equally (50/50) between the Parties, with each Party receiving approximately Three Hundred Thirty-Five Thousand Dollars (\$335,000) from the remaining balance. The Parties acknowledge that this reimbursement right reflects the traceable contribution of Wife\'s premarital savings and is intended to prevent the unjust enrichment of Husband at Wife\'s expense.

**Section 5.3 --- Valuation of Marital Property**

Marital Property shall be valued as of the date of Dissolution or such other date as the Parties may agree in writing or as a court of competent jurisdiction may order. The Parties may retain independent appraisers, valuators, or other qualified professionals as needed to determine the fair market value of any Marital Property. The Parties acknowledge that Pendleton Appraisal Group has established a baseline value for the Marital Residence and that Strand Valuation Services has established a baseline value for the Practice Interest, each as of the dates indicated in their respective reports. Updated valuations may be required at the time of any Dissolution proceeding.

**[ARTICLE 6: WIFE\'S PRACTICE INTEREST]{.underline}**

**Section 6.1 --- Classification**

Wife\'s twenty-two percent (22%) membership interest in Heartbeat Pediatric Partners LLC is classified as follows:

> **(a)** The value of the Practice Interest as of November 30, 2024, as determined by the valuation report of Strand Valuation Services in the amount of One Million Six Hundred Fifty Thousand Dollars (\$1,650,000), shall be Wife\'s Separate Property. This baseline value reflects the fair market value of the Practice Interest as of the date nearest to the Effective Date for which a reliable valuation has been obtained.
>
> **(b)** Any appreciation in the value of the Practice Interest above One Million Six Hundred Fifty Thousand Dollars (\$1,650,000) that accrues during the marriage after the Effective Date shall be classified as Marital Property and shall be subject to equitable division between the Parties under Section 5.1 of this Agreement. For the avoidance of doubt, all appreciation above the baseline value of \$1,650,000 shall be considered marital in character, regardless of the source or cause of such appreciation, and shall be divided equally (50/50) between the Parties upon Dissolution.

**Section 6.2 --- Valuation Upon Dissolution**

In the event of Dissolution, the Practice Interest shall be valued as of the date of Dissolution (or such other date as the Parties may agree or a court may order) by a qualified business valuation firm mutually agreed upon by the Parties. If the Parties are unable to agree on a valuation firm within thirty (30) days following the filing of a petition for dissolution, the court shall appoint a qualified business valuation professional. The costs of such valuation shall be shared equally by the Parties. The Parties acknowledge that Strand Valuation Services performed the baseline valuation of the Practice Interest and may be retained for the updated valuation if mutually agreed.

**Section 6.3 --- Restrictions on Transfer**

Wife shall not transfer, sell, assign, encumber, pledge, hypothecate, or otherwise dispose of the Practice Interest, or any portion thereof, without Husband\'s prior written consent if such action would materially impair the marital estate or reduce the value of the Practice Interest below the baseline value of One Million Six Hundred Fifty Thousand Dollars (\$1,650,000). Notwithstanding the foregoing, Wife\'s operational management of the Practice Interest in the ordinary course of business, including clinical practice decisions, administrative matters, and participation in the governance of Heartbeat Pediatric Partners LLC, shall not require Husband\'s consent.

**[ARTICLE 7: HUSBAND\'S RESTRICTED STOCK UNITS]{.underline}**

**Section 7.1 --- Classification of RSUs**

The twelve thousand (12,000) unvested restricted stock units of Grayfield Industries common stock granted to Husband on January 1, 2024, with a vesting schedule of four thousand (4,000) shares per year vesting on each of January 1, 2025, January 1, 2026, and January 1, 2027, are classified as Marital Property. The grant date of January 1, 2024, falls entirely within the marriage, and the entire vesting period of January 1, 2024, through December 31, 2027, falls within the marriage. Accordingly, the RSUs are entirely marital in character and are subject to division between the Parties as Marital Property.

**Section 7.2 --- Division of RSUs**

Upon vesting, each tranche of RSUs shall be divided equally between the Parties (fifty percent (50%) to Wife and fifty percent (50%) to Husband). In the event of Dissolution prior to full vesting, the unvested RSUs shall be divided equally (50/50) between the Parties, either by direct transfer of shares (to the extent permitted by Grayfield Industries\' equity plan and applicable securities laws), by establishment of a constructive trust for the benefit of the non-employee spouse, or by payment of the cash equivalent at the fair market value of the shares on the date of vesting or Dissolution, whichever is applicable, at the election of the Parties or as ordered by the court.

Based on the current fair market value of Thirty-Eight Dollars (\$38.00) per share, the aggregate value of the twelve thousand (12,000) RSUs is Four Hundred Fifty-Six Thousand Dollars (\$456,000). Upon equal division, each Party would receive the equivalent of six thousand (6,000) shares or Two Hundred Twenty-Eight Thousand Dollars (\$228,000) in value. The Parties acknowledge that the actual value at the time of vesting or Dissolution may differ from the current fair market value.

**Section 7.3 --- Tax Consequences**

The Parties acknowledge that the vesting of RSUs will create taxable income to the holder spouse (Husband) in the year of vesting. Tax liabilities arising from the vesting of RSUs shall be allocated pro rata to each Party based on their respective share of the RSUs. Husband shall be responsible for tax withholding obligations as required by Grayfield Industries\' equity compensation plan, and Wife shall reimburse Husband for Wife\'s pro rata share of taxes attributable to Wife\'s share of the vested RSUs within thirty (30) days of the applicable vesting date. The Parties shall cooperate in tax reporting and shall file tax returns in a manner consistent with the allocation of RSU income set forth in this Agreement.

**[ARTICLE 8: RETIREMENT ACCOUNTS]{.underline}**

**Section 8.1 --- Current Retirement Account Balances**

As of the Effective Date, the Parties hold the following retirement accounts:

> **(a)** Wife holds a 403(b) retirement savings plan and a defined-benefit pension plan through Lakeshore Children\'s Hospital, with a combined value of approximately Two Million Three Hundred Forty Thousand Dollars (\$2,340,000).
>
> **(b)** Husband holds a 401(k) retirement savings plan through Grayfield Industries, with a current value of approximately One Million One Hundred Eighty Thousand Dollars (\$1,180,000).
>
> **(c)** The total combined retirement account value of both Parties is approximately Three Million Five Hundred Twenty Thousand Dollars (\$3,520,000).

**Section 8.2 --- Pre-Agreement Balances as Quasi-Separate Property**

Each Party shall retain their respective retirement account balances accumulated as of the Effective Date as their own property, not subject to division upon Dissolution except as may be required by a Qualified Domestic Relations Order (QDRO) or equivalent mechanism necessitated by law. For the avoidance of doubt, Wife shall retain Two Million Three Hundred Forty Thousand Dollars (\$2,340,000) in her 403(b) and defined-benefit pension accounts, and Husband shall retain One Million One Hundred Eighty Thousand Dollars (\$1,180,000) in his 401(k) account, each representing the pre-Agreement balance in their respective accounts.

**Section 8.3 --- Future Contributions**

All contributions made by either Party to their respective retirement accounts after the Effective Date, and all appreciation, earnings, and investment returns thereon, shall be classified as Marital Property and shall be subject to equal (50/50) division upon Dissolution. Division of retirement accounts shall be effectuated through appropriate Qualified Domestic Relations Orders (QDROs) or equivalent mechanisms as required by the applicable plan documents and governing law, including the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974, as amended (ERISA), to the extent applicable.

**[ARTICLE 9: MARITAL RESIDENCE]{.underline}**

**Section 9.1 --- Description and Current Status**

The Marital Residence is the single-family home located at 1847 Birchwood Lane, Winnetka, Illinois 60093. The Marital Residence was purchased by the Parties in 2014 for One Million Three Hundred Fifty Thousand Dollars (\$1,350,000). The Marital Residence has been appraised at a current fair market value of One Million Eight Hundred Seventy-Five Thousand Dollars (\$1,875,000) by Pendleton Appraisal Group, as set forth in the appraisal report dated November 15, 2024. The Marital Residence is subject to an outstanding mortgage balance of Six Hundred Twelve Thousand Dollars (\$612,000), held by Prairie State Title Company. The resulting net equity in the Marital Residence is approximately One Million Two Hundred Sixty-Three Thousand Dollars (\$1,263,000).

**Section 9.2 --- Wife\'s Right of First Purchase**

Upon any Dissolution, Wife shall have the right of first refusal to purchase Husband\'s equity interest in the Marital Residence at its then-appraised fair market value (less the outstanding mortgage balance and any customary adjustments), as determined by a licensed appraiser mutually agreed upon by the Parties. If the Parties are unable to agree on an appraiser within fifteen (15) days following the entry of a dissolution judgment, each Party shall select one licensed appraiser, and the two appraisers so selected shall jointly select a third appraiser whose valuation shall be binding on the Parties. Wife shall have sixty (60) days from the entry of a dissolution judgment to exercise this right of first refusal by providing written notice to Husband. Payment of Husband\'s equity share shall be made within twenty-four (24) months of the exercise of the right, either by lump sum payment, refinancing of the existing mortgage, or structured installment payments with interest accruing at the applicable federal rate in effect on the date of exercise.

**Section 9.3 --- Sale if No Purchase**

If neither Party elects to purchase the other Party\'s interest in the Marital Residence within the time periods specified in Section 9.2, the Marital Residence shall be listed for sale at fair market value within thirty (30) days following the expiration of the purchase option period, and the net proceeds from such sale (after satisfaction of the outstanding mortgage, closing costs, real estate commissions, transfer taxes, and other customary expenses of sale) shall be divided equally (50/50) between the Parties.

**Section 9.4 --- Mortgage Obligations During Marriage**

During the marriage, the Parties shall continue to share mortgage payments, property taxes, homeowner\'s insurance, and reasonable maintenance and repair costs from marital funds or as they may otherwise agree in writing. Neither Party shall encumber, refinance, or otherwise pledge the Marital Residence as collateral for any obligation without the prior written consent of the other Party. Each Party shall have equal right to occupy and enjoy the Marital Residence during the marriage.

**[ARTICLE 10: SPOUSAL MAINTENANCE]{.underline}**

**Section 10.1 --- Right to Seek Maintenance**

In the event of Dissolution, the lower-earning spouse shall have the right to seek spousal maintenance in accordance with the Illinois Marriage and Dissolution of Marriage Act, 750 ILCS 5/504, subject to the limitations and provisions set forth in this Article. The Parties acknowledge that, as of the Effective Date, Husband is the lower-earning spouse, with total average annual compensation of approximately Three Hundred Five Thousand Dollars (\$305,000), as compared to Wife\'s annual W-2 compensation of Four Hundred Eighty-Seven Thousand Dollars (\$487,000).

**Section 10.2 --- Duration Cap**

Notwithstanding any statutory formula, guideline, or judicial discretion that might otherwise apply, spousal maintenance under this Agreement shall not exceed five (5) years from the date of entry of the dissolution judgment. The Parties agree that this five-year cap reflects the duration of the marriage (in excess of ten (10) years as of the Effective Date), the respective earning capacities and employment histories of the Parties, and the goal of providing a reasonable transitional period for the lower-earning spouse while promoting financial independence.

**Section 10.3 --- Calculation**

The amount of spousal maintenance, if awarded, shall be calculated in accordance with the statutory guidelines set forth in 750 ILCS 5/504(b-1), based on each Party\'s Gross Income as defined in Section 2.1(d) of this Agreement. The Parties acknowledge that Gross Income, as defined herein, includes all forms of compensation and income from any source, including but not limited to base salary, bonuses, equity compensation, and investment income.

**Section 10.4 --- Termination Events**

Spousal maintenance shall terminate upon the earliest to occur of any of the following events:

> **(a)** The death of either Party;
>
> **(b)** The remarriage of the receiving Party;
>
> **(c)** The cohabitation of the receiving Party with another person on a resident, continuing, conjugal basis, as defined by Illinois law and interpreted by Illinois courts; or
>
> **(d)** The expiration of the five (5)-year duration cap set forth in Section 10.2 of this Agreement.

Upon the occurrence of any termination event, the obligation to pay spousal maintenance shall cease immediately, and neither Party shall have any further obligation to the other with respect to spousal maintenance under this Agreement.

**[ARTICLE 11: DEBT ALLOCATION]{.underline}**

**Section 11.1 --- Husband\'s Undisclosed Credit Card Debt**

The Parties acknowledge that Husband incurred approximately Sixty-Eight Thousand Dollars (\$68,000) in credit card debt (the \"Identified Debt\") during the marriage, the existence of which was not disclosed to Wife during the period of its accumulation and was first revealed to Wife in mid-2024. Husband shall assume sole and exclusive responsibility for the Identified Debt and shall hold Wife harmless from and indemnify Wife against any and all claims, demands, actions, liabilities, losses, costs, and expenses (including reasonable attorney\'s fees and court costs) arising from or related to the Identified Debt, without limitation as to the amount or nature of individual claims. Husband shall use his best efforts to satisfy the Identified Debt in full as expeditiously as practicable. Wife shall have no obligation to contribute to the payment or satisfaction of the Identified Debt, and any payment by Wife toward the Identified Debt shall be subject to a right of reimbursement from Husband.

**Section 11.2 --- Other Debts**

Except as provided in Section 11.1, the Parties shall be jointly responsible for debts incurred by mutual agreement during the marriage and from which both Parties have benefited. Neither Party shall incur individual debt exceeding Ten Thousand Dollars (\$10,000) without the prior written consent of the other Party. Any debt incurred by a Party in violation of this Section shall be the sole responsibility of the incurring Party, and the other Party shall have no obligation with respect thereto. The Parties shall cooperate in the management of marital debts and shall make reasonable efforts to minimize the incurrence of new obligations.

**Section 11.3 --- Future Disclosure of Debts**

Each Party shall promptly disclose to the other Party any new debt exceeding Five Thousand Dollars (\$5,000) incurred after the Effective Date, regardless of whether such debt was incurred individually or jointly. Disclosure shall be made in writing within fifteen (15) days of incurrence. Failure to disclose any such debt shall constitute a material breach of this Agreement and may give rise to remedies under Article 13.

**[ARTICLE 12: FINANCIAL DISCLOSURE]{.underline}**

**Section 12.1 --- Incorporated Disclosures**

The financial disclosure schedules prepared by the certified public accounting firm of Linden & Fosse CPA, attached hereto as Exhibit A (Wife\'s Financial Disclosure) and Exhibit B (Husband\'s Financial Disclosure), are incorporated by reference into this Agreement and form an integral part hereof. Each Party represents and warrants that the information contained in their respective financial disclosure schedule is true, complete, and accurate in all material respects as of the date of preparation.

**Section 12.2 --- Independent Review**

Each Party has had the opportunity to independently review, examine, and verify the other Party\'s financial disclosures and to engage their own financial advisors, certified public accountants, appraisers, or other professionals for the purpose of such review and verification. Each Party acknowledges that they have either conducted such independent review or have voluntarily elected not to do so, and that in either case they are satisfied with the adequacy of the disclosures made.

**Section 12.3 --- Voidability**

As set forth more fully in Section 3.3 of this Agreement, this Agreement is voidable if material omissions or misrepresentations are discovered in either Party\'s financial disclosures. The right to seek voidability under Section 3.3 is not limited by any heightened evidentiary standard, dollar threshold, or requirement of intent on the part of the non-disclosing Party. The provisions of Section 3.3 are hereby incorporated by reference into this Article.

**[ARTICLE 13: ENFORCEMENT AND ATTORNEY\'S FEES]{.underline}**

**Section 13.1 --- Enforcement**

This Agreement shall be enforceable in any court of competent jurisdiction. Either Party may seek specific performance, injunctive relief, compensatory damages, or any other remedy available at law or in equity to enforce the terms and provisions of this Agreement. The Parties agree that the terms of this Agreement are reasonable and enforceable and that the remedies available at law may be inadequate in certain circumstances, making equitable relief appropriate.

**Section 13.2 --- Prevailing Party Attorney\'s Fees**

In any action, proceeding, or dispute to enforce, interpret, or declare rights under this Agreement, including but not limited to any proceeding incident to a Dissolution in which the terms of this Agreement are at issue, the prevailing party shall be entitled to recover their reasonable attorney\'s fees, court costs, expert witness fees, and other litigation expenses from the non-prevailing party. The determination of the prevailing party and the reasonableness of fees and costs shall be made by the court in which such action or proceeding is pending.

**[ARTICLE 14: MODIFICATION AND AMENDMENT]{.underline}**

**Section 14.1 --- Mutual Written Consent Required**

This Agreement may be modified, amended, supplemented, or waived only by a written instrument executed by both Parties with the same formality as this Agreement. No modification, amendment, supplement, or waiver of any term or provision of this Agreement shall be effective unless it is in writing and bears the signatures of both Wife and Husband. Oral modifications, amendments, or waivers are expressly prohibited and shall have no legal force or effect. Any purported unilateral modification, amendment, or waiver by either Party --- whether oral, written, or implied by conduct --- shall be void and of no effect.

**[ARTICLE 15: GENERAL PROVISIONS]{.underline}**

**Section 15.1 --- Governing Law**

This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Illinois, including without limitation the Illinois Marriage and Dissolution of Marriage Act, 750 ILCS 5/502 (agreements in contemplation of or during marriage), 750 ILCS 5/503 (disposition of property and debts), and 750 ILCS 5/504 (maintenance), and all other applicable provisions of Illinois statutory and common law.

**Section 15.2 --- Venue and Forum Selection**

Any action, proceeding, or dispute arising under or relating to this Agreement, or the enforcement, interpretation, or validity thereof, shall be brought exclusively in the Circuit Court of Cook County, Illinois, and each Party hereby consents to the personal jurisdiction of such court and waives any objection to venue in such court.

**Section 15.3 --- Entire Agreement**

This Agreement, together with the Exhibits and Schedules attached hereto and incorporated herein by reference, constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior negotiations, representations, understandings, or agreements between the Parties, whether written or oral, relating to the subject matter of this Agreement.

**Section 15.4 --- Sunset Clause**

This Agreement shall automatically terminate and be of no further force or effect on June 14, 2040, which date is fifteen (15) years from the anticipated execution date, unless the Parties renew this Agreement in writing prior to such date. Upon termination of this Agreement under this Section, all rights, obligations, and classifications established herein shall cease, and the Parties\' respective property rights shall be governed solely by applicable Illinois law as in effect at such time. The Parties may renew this Agreement for additional periods of five (5) years each by mutual written consent executed no later than ninety (90) days prior to the then-applicable expiration date. Any renewal shall be in writing, signed by both Parties, and may incorporate such modifications or amendments as the Parties may agree upon at the time of renewal.

**Section 15.5 --- Counterparts**

This Agreement may be executed in one or more counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original and all of which, taken together, shall constitute one and the same instrument. Facsimile and electronic signatures shall be deemed original signatures for purposes of this Agreement.

**Section 15.6 --- Waiver**

The failure of either Party to enforce any provision of this Agreement at any time shall not constitute a waiver of such provision or of the right to enforce such provision at any time thereafter. No waiver of any breach of any term of this Agreement shall be construed as a waiver of any subsequent breach.

**Section 15.7 --- Headings**

The Article and Section headings contained in this Agreement are for convenience of reference only and shall not affect the meaning, interpretation, or construction of any provision of this Agreement.

**Section 15.8 --- Independent Legal Counsel**

Each Party acknowledges and represents that they have been represented by independent legal counsel of their own choosing in the negotiation, preparation, and execution of this Agreement. Each Party further acknowledges that they have read this Agreement in its entirety, that they understand its terms and provisions, and that they execute this Agreement voluntarily, freely, and without duress, coercion, or undue influence from the other Party, from counsel, or from any third party.

**Section 15.9 --- Voluntary Execution and Fairness**

Each Party represents and warrants that this Agreement is fair and equitable at the time of execution, taking into account the respective financial circumstances, earning capacities, needs, and contributions of each Party. Neither Party has been subjected to fraud, duress, overreaching, or any other improper conduct by the other Party or any third party in connection with the negotiation or execution of this Agreement. Both Parties acknowledge the fiduciary relationship that exists between spouses under Illinois law and represent that they have dealt with each other in the utmost good faith and candor throughout the process leading to the execution of this Agreement.

**Section 15.10 --- No Pending Proceedings**

No action for dissolution of marriage, legal separation, annulment, or declaration of invalidity of marriage is pending as of the Effective Date in the Circuit Court of Cook County, Illinois, or in any other court of competent jurisdiction. Neither Party has filed or caused to be filed any such action, and neither Party is aware of any intention by the other Party to file such an action as of the Effective Date.

**\[SIGNATURE PAGE FOLLOWS\]**

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Parties have executed this Postnuptial Agreement as of the Effective Date first written above.

**WIFE:**

\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

DIANE VESELKA-THORNTON

**HUSBAND:**

\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

MARCUS THORNTON

**APPROVED AS TO FORM:**

\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

Catherine Odom Kessler, Odom & Pratt LLP 210 South Wacker Drive, Suite 2400 Chicago, Illinois 60606 Counsel for Wife
