result:
**[SUMMARY VALUATION REPORT]{.underline}**

**[22% Membership Interest in Heartbeat Pediatric Partners LLC]{.underline}**

**An Illinois Limited Liability Company**

Prepared by:

**STRAND VALUATION SERVICES** 135 South LaSalle Street, Suite 3100 Chicago, Illinois 60603

Prepared for:

**Catherine Odom, Partner** Kessler, Odom & Pratt LLP 200 South Wacker Drive, Suite 2400 Chicago, Illinois 60606

On Behalf of:

**Diane Veselka-Thornton**

**Valuation Date: November 30, 2024**

**Report Date: November 30, 2024**

*Engagement Type: Summary valuation report for use in connection with postnuptial agreement negotiations*

**CONFIDENTIALITY NOTICE:** This report is intended solely for the use of the client, Diane Veselka-Thornton, and her legal counsel, Kessler, Odom & Pratt LLP, in connection with the above-referenced matter. This report should not be distributed, reproduced, or used for any other purpose without the prior written consent of Strand Valuation Services. The information contained herein is privileged and confidential.

**[SECTION 1: LETTER OF TRANSMITTAL]{.underline}**

November 30, 2024

Catherine Odom, Partner Kessler, Odom & Pratt LLP 200 South Wacker Drive, Suite 2400 Chicago, Illinois 60606

Dear Ms. Odom:

At your request and pursuant to our engagement agreement dated September 16, 2024, Strand Valuation Services has performed an independent valuation of a 22% membership interest in Heartbeat Pediatric Partners LLC, an Illinois limited liability company engaged in the practice of pediatric cardiology and general pediatric medicine in suburban Chicago, Illinois. This engagement was undertaken on behalf of your client, Diane Veselka-Thornton, for purposes of the negotiation and execution of a postnuptial agreement.

Based upon our analyses, and subject to the assumptions, limiting conditions, and qualifications set forth in this report, it is our opinion that the fair market value of the 22% membership interest held by Dr. Veselka-Thornton in Heartbeat Pediatric Partners LLC, as of November 30, 2024, is:

**\$1,650,000** **(One Million Six Hundred Fifty Thousand Dollars)**

This report is a summary valuation report prepared in accordance with the professional standards of the American Society of Appraisers and the Uniform Standards of Professional Appraisal Practice (USPAP). A summary report does not include all of the analyses, data, and supporting documentation that would be present in a comprehensive (detailed) report. Supporting workpapers and documentation are maintained in our files and are available upon request.

We appreciate the opportunity to be of service in this matter.

Respectfully submitted,

**STRAND VALUATION SERVICES**

By: **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_**

Robert M. Strand, ASA, CFA Senior Managing Director Strand Valuation Services

**[SECTION 2: PURPOSE AND SCOPE OF ENGAGEMENT]{.underline}**

Strand Valuation Services was retained by the law firm of Kessler, Odom & Pratt LLP, on behalf of its client Diane Veselka-Thornton, to provide an independent opinion of the fair market value of Dr. Veselka-Thornton\'s 22% membership interest in Heartbeat Pediatric Partners LLC (hereinafter, the \"Practice\" or \"Heartbeat Pediatric Partners\"). The purpose of this valuation is for use in the negotiation and execution of a postnuptial agreement between Diane Veselka-Thornton (age 44), a board-certified pediatric cardiologist, and her husband, Marcus Thornton (age 46), Senior Vice President of Business Development at Grayfield Industries, a publicly traded industrial conglomerate. The parties were married on June 14, 2013, and currently reside at 1847 Birchwood Lane, Winnetka, Illinois 60093.

**Valuation Date.** The effective date of this valuation is November 30, 2024. All analyses, conclusions, and opinions expressed herein are as of that date.

**Standard of Value.** The standard of value applied in this engagement is fair market value, defined as the price at which property would change hands between a willing buyer and a willing seller, neither being under compulsion to buy or sell, and both having reasonable knowledge of relevant facts. This definition is consistent with the standard articulated by the Internal Revenue Service in Revenue Ruling 59-60 and is widely accepted in both legal and valuation professional contexts.

**Premise of Value.** The valuation was performed under the premise of value as a going concern --- that is, the Practice is assumed to continue operating in its current form for the foreseeable future.

**Scope of Review.** In the course of this engagement, Strand Valuation Services reviewed and considered the following sources of information, among others:

> • Audited and internally prepared financial statements of Heartbeat Pediatric Partners LLC for fiscal years 2019 through 2023, and unaudited financial data through October 2024;
>
> • Federal income tax returns and associated Schedule K-1s for the Practice for fiscal years 2019 through 2023;
>
> • The Amended and Restated Operating Agreement of Heartbeat Pediatric Partners LLC, dated March 15, 2018;
>
> • Provider compensation surveys and benchmarking data from the Medical Group Management Association (MGMA) and the American Medical Group Association (AMGA);
>
> • Payer mix reports and managed care contract summaries for the Practice;
>
> • Industry publications and market data relating to pediatric cardiology and physician-owned specialty practices;
>
> • Interviews with Dr. Veselka-Thornton and the Practice\'s managing partner regarding operations, growth prospects, competitive dynamics, and strategic planning; and
>
> • Financial disclosures prepared in coordination with Linden & Fosse CPA, the certified public accounting firm retained to prepare financial disclosures for both parties in connection with the postnuptial agreement process.

We note that certain financial data and disclosures were coordinated through Linden & Fosse CPA, and we have relied upon the accuracy and completeness of the information provided by that firm and by the Practice\'s management. Strand Valuation Services has not independently audited or verified the financial information supplied to us.

**[SECTION 3: DESCRIPTION OF THE ENTITY]{.underline}**

**[Overview]{.underline}**

Heartbeat Pediatric Partners LLC is an Illinois limited liability company organized as a physician-owned medical practice. The Practice provides a comprehensive range of pediatric cardiology and general pediatric services to patients throughout suburban Chicago, Illinois, and the greater Chicagoland metropolitan area. The Practice was founded in approximately 2012 and has been in continuous operation for approximately twelve years as of the valuation date.

**[Services and Clinical Operations]{.underline}**

The Practice\'s core clinical services include pediatric cardiology consultations, diagnostic evaluation and management of congenital and acquired heart disease in children, and ongoing cardiac care coordination. In addition to its cardiology subspecialty, the Practice provides general pediatric services, including well-child examinations, acute illness management, and developmental assessments. A significant component of the Practice\'s revenue is derived from ancillary diagnostic services, including echocardiography (transthoracic and fetal), exercise stress testing, Holter and event monitoring, and electrocardiography. These diagnostic services are performed in-house at both of the Practice\'s clinical locations, providing convenience for patients and an important revenue stream for the Practice.

**[Hospital Affiliation]{.underline}**

The Practice operates in affiliation with Lakeshore Children\'s Hospital, a prominent pediatric institution in suburban Chicago. Several of the Practice\'s physicians, including Dr. Veselka-Thornton, hold active clinical privileges at Lakeshore Children\'s Hospital and provide inpatient consultative services, participate in hospital-based cardiac programs, and contribute to multidisciplinary pediatric care teams. This hospital affiliation is a material component of the Practice\'s referral network and revenue generation.

**[Ownership Structure]{.underline}**

Heartbeat Pediatric Partners LLC is structured as a multi-physician limited liability company with several physician-members. Dr. Diane Veselka-Thornton holds a 22% membership interest in the LLC. The remaining membership interests are held by the other physician-partners. The Practice\'s current physician complement includes approximately eight to ten physicians --- a combination of pediatric cardiologists and general pediatricians --- supported by nurse practitioners, medical assistants, registered nurses, administrative staff, and billing personnel.

**[Locations]{.underline}**

The Practice operates from two clinical locations in suburban Cook County, Illinois. Both locations are leased premises, and the leases are held in the name of the Practice.

**[Operating Agreement Provisions Relevant to Valuation]{.underline}**

The Amended and Restated Operating Agreement of Heartbeat Pediatric Partners LLC, dated March 15, 2018, contains several provisions material to the valuation of a minority membership interest. These include:

> • **Restrictions on Transfer.** Membership interests may not be transferred, assigned, or encumbered without the prior written consent of members holding at least two-thirds of the outstanding membership interests. Any proposed transfer is subject to a right of first refusal by the remaining members, exercisable within ninety days of written notice.
>
> • **Non-Compete Covenants.** Departing members are subject to a non-competition covenant restricting the practice of pediatric cardiology within a defined geographic radius for a period of two years following departure.
>
> • **Mandatory Buy-Sell Provisions.** The Operating Agreement contains mandatory buy-sell provisions triggered upon the death, permanent disability, or voluntary withdrawal of a member. The buy-sell price is determined by a formula based on a trailing three-year average of the Practice\'s net revenues, subject to adjustment by mutual agreement or independent appraisal.

These transfer restrictions and buy-sell provisions are relevant factors in the determination of appropriate valuation discounts, as discussed in Section 7 of this report.

**[SECTION 4: ECONOMIC AND INDUSTRY OVERVIEW]{.underline}**

**[General Economic Conditions]{.underline}**

As of November 2024, the United States economy continues to experience moderate growth, with real GDP expanding at an annualized rate of approximately 2.5% in the third quarter of 2024. The Federal Reserve has maintained a cautious posture with respect to monetary policy, and inflation has moderated toward the central bank\'s target. The suburban Chicago metropolitan area has experienced economic conditions broadly consistent with national trends, with low unemployment, stable consumer spending, and continued growth in the healthcare sector.

**[Healthcare Industry Landscape]{.underline}**

The healthcare services industry in the greater Chicagoland area remains robust, characterized by steady demand for physician services, ongoing consolidation among hospital systems and physician groups, and gradual shifts in reimbursement models from fee-for-service toward value-based care arrangements. Physician-owned specialty practices in the suburbs of Chicago continue to operate in a favorable demand environment, supported by population growth, an aging demographic profile among certain patient populations, and expanded insurance coverage under the Affordable Care Act and state Medicaid programs.

**[Pediatric Cardiology Market Conditions]{.underline}**

The pediatric cardiology subspecialty occupies a favorable position within the broader healthcare services market. Demand for pediatric cardiology services in the suburban Chicago market has grown steadily over the review period, driven by several factors:

**Insurance Reimbursement Rate Increases.** Over the five-year period from 2019 through 2024, commercial insurance reimbursement rates for pediatric cardiology services --- including evaluation and management codes, echocardiography, and other diagnostic procedures --- have increased by approximately 12% to 18% nationally. These rate increases have been driven by annual adjustments to the Medicare Physician Fee Schedule published by the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS), commercial insurer benchmarking to CMS rates, and renegotiation of managed care contracts at the regional level. These are industry-wide phenomena reflecting systemic changes in payer reimbursement, and they are not attributable to the marketing, negotiation, or clinical efforts of any individual physician practice.

**Demographic Market Expansion.** The suburban communities of Cook County, Illinois, and adjacent collar counties have experienced meaningful population growth in the family-age demographic --- households with children aged 0 through 18 --- over the review period. According to data from the U.S. Census Bureau and the Illinois Department of Public Health, the pediatric population in the Practice\'s primary service area grew by approximately 6% to 8% between 2019 and 2024. This demographic expansion has resulted in increased patient volumes for pediatric specialty practices, independent of any individual practice\'s physician recruitment or marketing initiatives.

**Ancillary Diagnostic Service Demand.** Utilization of echocardiographic studies and other cardiac diagnostic procedures in the pediatric population has increased in response to evolving clinical practice guidelines issued by the American Academy of Pediatrics and the American College of Cardiology, expanded screening recommendations for student-athletes, and greater awareness among primary care providers of the need for early cardiac evaluation. These trends have contributed to increased diagnostic volumes across the pediatric cardiology sector.

**[Regulatory Environment]{.underline}**

No material adverse regulatory changes affecting the Practice\'s operations or reimbursement were identified during the review period. The Practice maintains compliance with all applicable state licensure, credentialing, and billing regulations.

**[Competitive Environment]{.underline}**

Direct competition in pediatric cardiology within the Practice\'s service area is limited. The number of board-certified pediatric cardiologists in suburban Cook County is relatively small, and barriers to entry are significant due to the subspecialty credentialing requirements, the need for hospital affiliations, and the capital investment required for in-house diagnostic equipment. These competitive dynamics support the Practice\'s continued market position and are favorable factors in the valuation.

**[SECTION 5: FINANCIAL ANALYSIS]{.underline}**

**[Summary of Financial Performance]{.underline}**

The following table presents a summary of the Practice\'s financial performance for the five-year period from 2019 through 2024. The 2024 figures are annualized based on actual results through October 31, 2024.

  ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Fiscal Year**   **Total Revenue**   **Operating Expenses**   **EBITDA**     **Operating Margin**
  ----------------- ------------------- ------------------------ -------------- ----------------------
  2019              \$8,200,000         \$5,820,000              \$2,380,000    29.0%

  2020              \$7,950,000         \$5,730,000              \$2,220,000    27.9%

  2021              \$9,400,000         \$6,580,000              \$2,820,000    30.0%

  2022              \$10,600,000        \$7,420,000              \$3,180,000    30.0%

  2023              \$11,800,000        \$8,260,000              \$3,540,000    30.0%

  2024 (Ann.)       \$12,800,000        \$9,090,000              \$3,710,000    29.0%
  ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Total practice revenues grew from approximately \$8.2 million in fiscal year 2019 to approximately \$12.8 million on an annualized basis for fiscal year 2024. This represents a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of approximately 9.3% over the five-year period. We note that fiscal year 2020 experienced a slight decline in revenue attributable to the temporary impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on elective and non-emergent clinical volumes; however, the Practice recovered quickly in 2021 and has sustained strong growth through the valuation date.

**[Revenue Growth Attribution Analysis]{.underline}**

In connection with this engagement, Strand Valuation Services performed a decomposition analysis to identify and quantify the principal sources of the Practice\'s revenue growth over the five-year review period. This analysis was undertaken because the distinction between active and passive sources of growth may be relevant for purposes of marital property classification under Illinois law.

**Passive Factors --- Approximately 60% of Revenue Growth.** Our analysis indicates that approximately 60% of the Practice\'s revenue growth during the review period is attributable to passive, market-driven factors that are external to the active management or clinical efforts of any individual physician-partner:

> \(i\) *Insurance Reimbursement Rate Increases* (approximately 35% of total revenue growth): Commercial and governmental payer rates for pediatric cardiology evaluation and management services, echocardiography, and other diagnostic procedures increased approximately 12% to 18% nationally between 2019 and 2024. These increases were driven by CMS rate-setting adjustments, commercial insurer benchmarking practices, and regulatory and legislative changes at the state and federal levels. These rate increases flow through to all practices providing the relevant services and are not attributable to the active negotiation or management efforts of any individual physician-partner at Heartbeat Pediatric Partners.
>
> \(ii\) *Demographic Market Expansion* (approximately 25% of total revenue growth): Population growth in the Practice\'s primary service area --- specifically, growth in the 0--18 age demographic in suburban Cook County --- resulted in increased patient volumes for pediatric specialty services without incremental physician recruitment or marketing efforts by the Practice. This demographic expansion is an external, market-level factor reflecting broader population trends in the Chicagoland metropolitan area.

**Active Factors --- Approximately 40% of Revenue Growth.** The remaining approximately 40% of revenue growth is attributable to active factors involving the deliberate management decisions and clinical efforts of the physician-owners:

> \(i\) *Physician Recruitment* (approximately 25% of total revenue growth): During the review period, the Practice recruited two new physician-partners and one nurse practitioner, expanding clinical capacity and enabling the Practice to serve a larger patient population. These recruitment decisions were made collectively by the existing partners, including Dr. Veselka-Thornton, and required active management involvement in candidate identification, credentialing, onboarding, and integration.
>
> \(ii\) *Operational Improvements* (approximately 15% of total revenue growth): The Practice implemented a new electronic health records (EHR) system, expanded its in-house ancillary diagnostic service offerings, and renegotiated the terms of its hospital affiliation agreements with Lakeshore Children\'s Hospital. These operational initiatives required active management involvement by the physician-owners and contributed to improved clinical efficiency, enhanced revenue capture, and expanded service capabilities.

**[Operating Margins]{.underline}**

The Practice maintains operating margins in the range of approximately 28% to 32% over the review period, which is consistent with industry benchmarks for physician-owned specialty practices as reported by the MGMA and similar surveys. The operating margin profile reflects a stable cost structure, effective revenue cycle management, and favorable payer contracts.

**[Normalized Earnings]{.underline}**

After adjusting for above-market physician compensation relative to MGMA survey medians, one-time expenses (including EHR implementation costs in 2021 and litigation settlement costs in 2022), and non-recurring items, normalized earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization (EBITDA) for the trailing twelve months ended October 31, 2024, is approximately \$3,400,000 for the Practice as a whole. Additional adjustments were made for estimated maintenance capital expenditure requirements and working capital needs to arrive at normalized net cash flow, as described in Section 6.

**[Dr. Veselka-Thornton\'s Compensation and Distributions]{.underline}**

Dr. Veselka-Thornton receives annual W-2 compensation of approximately \$487,000 from Lakeshore Children\'s Hospital, where she is employed as a pediatric cardiologist on the hospital\'s medical staff. In addition to her hospital salary, her 22% membership interest in Heartbeat Pediatric Partners LLC entitles her to periodic cash distributions from the Practice. Annual distributions from the Practice to Dr. Veselka-Thornton have averaged approximately \$145,000 over the most recent three-year period (2021 through 2023).

**[Balance Sheet Summary]{.underline}**

As of October 31, 2024, the Practice holds approximately \$1,800,000 in tangible assets, consisting primarily of medical and diagnostic equipment, leasehold improvements, and cash and cash equivalents. Total liabilities are approximately \$600,000, consisting of equipment financing obligations, accounts payable, and accrued expenses. The net tangible book value of the Practice is approximately \$1,200,000. The book value significantly understates the enterprise value of the Practice due to the presence of substantial intangible assets, as discussed in the following sections.

**[SECTION 6: VALUATION METHODOLOGY]{.underline}**

**[Approaches Considered]{.underline}**

In determining the fair market value of the Practice\'s enterprise, Strand Valuation Services considered the three generally accepted approaches to business valuation: (1) the Income Approach, (2) the Market Approach, and (3) the Asset-Based Approach.

**[Income Approach --- Capitalization of Earnings (Primary Method, Weighted 60%)]{.underline}**

Under the Income Approach, value is estimated by converting an expected stream of economic benefits into a present value. For the Practice, we applied a capitalization of earnings methodology, which is appropriate for a mature, stable business with a predictable earnings profile.

We began with normalized EBITDA of approximately \$3,400,000 and made adjustments for estimated income taxes at an effective rate consistent with a pass-through entity, maintenance capital expenditures of approximately \$350,000 per year, and incremental working capital requirements. These adjustments yielded a normalized net cash flow available to equity holders of approximately \$2,400,000.

We applied a capitalization rate of 22% to the normalized net cash flow stream. The capitalization rate was developed using the build-up method, incorporating a risk-free rate, an equity risk premium, a size premium, an industry risk adjustment, and a company-specific risk premium. The selected capitalization rate reflects the risks inherent in a physician-owned specialty practice, including reliance on professional services, payer concentration, and regulatory risk, while also reflecting the Practice\'s favorable competitive position and stable earnings history.

The resulting indicated enterprise value under the Income Approach is approximately **\$10,800,000** (\$2,400,000 ÷ 0.22 ≈ \$10,909,000, rounded to \$10,800,000).

**[Market Approach --- Guideline Transaction Method (Secondary Method, Weighted 40%)]{.underline}**

Under the Market Approach, value is estimated by reference to observed transaction prices for comparable businesses. Strand Valuation Services identified and analyzed six transactions involving physician-owned specialty practices in the Midwest region completed between 2021 and 2024. These transactions involved practices with characteristics reasonably comparable to Heartbeat Pediatric Partners, including subspecialty focus, multi-physician structure, and suburban market positioning.

From the comparable transactions, we derived the following valuation multiples:

  -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Multiple**            **Range**               **Selected**
  ----------------------- ----------------------- -----------------------
  Revenue Multiple        1.3x -- 1.8x            1.5x

  EBITDA Multiple         3.5x -- 5.0x            4.2x
  -----------------------------------------------------------------------

Applying the selected revenue multiple of 1.5x to the Practice\'s normalized revenues (adjusted for comparability) and the selected EBITDA multiple of 4.2x to normalized EBITDA, and weighting the resulting indications, the Market Approach yields an indicated enterprise value of approximately **\$10,100,000**.

**[Asset-Based Approach]{.underline}**

The Asset-Based Approach estimates value by reference to the fair market value of the entity\'s underlying assets, net of liabilities. While this approach was considered, it was given minimal weight in the final conclusion. The Practice is a service-based going concern whose value is derived primarily from intangible assets --- including patient relationships, referral networks, assembled workforce, and practice goodwill --- that are not reflected on the balance sheet. The net tangible book value of approximately \$1,200,000 significantly understates the enterprise value of the Practice and does not provide a meaningful indication of fair market value on a going-concern basis.

**[Weighted Enterprise Value Conclusion]{.underline}**

The Income Approach and Market Approach were weighted as follows to arrive at a final indication of enterprise value:

  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Approach**         **Indicated Value**   **Weight**        **Weighted Value**
  -------------------- --------------------- ----------------- --------------------
  Income Approach      \$10,800,000          60%               \$6,480,000

  Market Approach      \$10,100,000          40%               \$4,040,000

  **Weighted Total**                                           **\$10,520,000**
  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Rounding to the nearest \$100,000, the concluded enterprise value of Heartbeat Pediatric Partners LLC is approximately **\$10,500,000**.

**[SECTION 7: DISCOUNTS AND ADJUSTMENTS]{.underline}**

**[Pro Rata Share]{.underline}**

Dr. Veselka-Thornton\'s 22% membership interest in Heartbeat Pediatric Partners LLC represents a proportionate share of the Practice\'s total enterprise value. On a pro rata basis, the value of the 22% interest is calculated as follows:

> \$10,500,000 × 22% = **\$2,310,000** (pro rata value before discounts)

**[Discount for Lack of Marketability (DLOM)]{.underline}**

The 22% membership interest in Heartbeat Pediatric Partners LLC is not publicly traded and is subject to significant restrictions on transfer under the Operating Agreement, including a right of first refusal by the remaining members and a mandatory buy-sell provision. These restrictions substantially limit the holder\'s ability to liquidate the interest in a timely manner at full value. Based on our analysis of restricted stock studies, pre-IPO discount studies, and the specific transfer restrictions applicable to this interest, we applied a Discount for Lack of Marketability of **20%**.

> \$2,310,000 × (1 − 0.20) = **\$1,848,000**

**[Discount for Lack of Control (DLOC)]{.underline}**

Dr. Veselka-Thornton holds a minority (22%) membership interest and does not have the ability to unilaterally direct the Practice\'s operations, distribution policies, or strategic decisions. As a minority interest holder, she is subject to the decisions of the majority with respect to most material governance matters. However, her interest is a meaningful minority --- sufficient to exert influence on certain decisions --- and the Operating Agreement includes certain protective provisions for minority members, including requirements for supermajority approval on specified matters. Considering these factors, we applied a Discount for Lack of Control of approximately **10.7%**.

> \$1,848,000 × (1 − 0.107) = **\$1,650,264**, rounded to **\$1,650,000**

**[Summary of Discounts]{.underline}**

  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Item**                                **Value**
  --------------------------------------- -----------------------------------
  Enterprise Value                        \$10,500,000

  Pro Rata Share (22%)                    \$2,310,000

  Less: DLOM (20%)                        (\$462,000)

  Subtotal After DLOM                     \$1,848,000

  Less: DLOC (10.7%)                      (\$198,000)

  **Fair Market Value of 22% Interest**   **\$1,650,000**
  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

The combined discount from the pro rata value is approximately 28.6% (\$660,000 ÷ \$2,310,000 = 28.6%). We note that no separate discount for key-person risk was applied, as the Practice is a multi-physician entity that is not dependent on the clinical activity or referral relationships of any single physician-partner.

**[SECTION 8: CONCLUSION OF VALUE]{.underline}**

Based on the analyses performed and described herein, and subject to the assumptions, limiting conditions, and qualifications stated in this report, it is the opinion of Strand Valuation Services that the fair market value of Dr. Diane Veselka-Thornton\'s 22% membership interest in Heartbeat Pediatric Partners LLC, as of the valuation date of **November 30, 2024**, is:

**\$1,650,000**

**(One Million Six Hundred Fifty Thousand Dollars)**

This conclusion of value represents the interest on a minority, non-marketable basis. The conclusion reflects the application of the Income Approach (capitalization of earnings, weighted 60%) and the Market Approach (guideline transaction method, weighted 40%), with appropriate discounts for lack of marketability (20%) and lack of control (approximately 10.7%) applied to the pro rata share of enterprise value. The combined discount from pro rata value is approximately 28.6%.

This opinion of value is valid only as of the stated valuation date and should not be relied upon for any purpose other than the postnuptial agreement negotiations described herein.

**[SECTION 9: SOURCES OF PRACTICE VALUE --- ACTIVE VERSUS PASSIVE COMPONENTS]{.underline}**

**[Purpose of Analysis]{.underline}**

In addition to the determination of fair market value, Strand Valuation Services was asked to provide an analysis of the components of value and growth attributable to active versus passive factors. This distinction may be relevant for purposes of marital property classification under Illinois law, where the characterization of an asset\'s appreciation as marital or non-marital property may depend, in part, on whether the appreciation is attributable to the active personal efforts of a spouse or to passive market forces and external economic conditions.

**[Summary of Findings]{.underline}**

The Practice\'s total enterprise value of approximately \$10,500,000 is supported by both tangible and intangible assets. The intangible value --- comprising established patient relationships, physician referral networks, an assembled and trained workforce, favorable payer contracts, clinical protocols and operational systems, and practice goodwill --- constitutes the substantial majority of the Practice\'s enterprise value. The net tangible book value of \$1,200,000 represents only approximately 11.4% of the total enterprise value, with intangible assets and goodwill accounting for the remaining approximately 88.6%.

**[Passive Components of Value and Growth]{.underline}**

Approximately **60%** of the Practice\'s revenue growth over the five-year review period (2019--2024) is attributable to passive, market-driven factors that are external to the active management or clinical efforts of any individual physician-partner:

> \(i\) *Insurance Reimbursement Rate Increases* (approximately 35% of total revenue growth): Commercial and governmental payer rates for pediatric cardiology services --- including evaluation and management codes (CPT 99201--99215), echocardiography procedures (CPT 93303--93325), and cardiac monitoring services --- increased approximately 12% to 18% nationally between 2019 and 2024. These rate increases were driven by annual CMS rate-setting adjustments to the Medicare Physician Fee Schedule, commercial insurer benchmarking to CMS rates, and legislative and regulatory changes at both the federal and state levels. The rate increases flow through systematically to all practices providing the relevant services and are not attributable to the active negotiation, management, or clinical efforts of any individual physician-partner at Heartbeat Pediatric Partners.
>
> \(ii\) *Demographic Market Expansion* (approximately 25% of total revenue growth): The suburban Cook County communities within the Practice\'s primary service area experienced population growth in the 0--18 age demographic of approximately 6% to 8% during the review period. This demographic expansion resulted in increased patient volumes for pediatric specialty services --- including new patient referrals, return visit volumes, and diagnostic procedure utilization --- without incremental marketing expenditures or physician recruitment by the Practice. The patient volume growth attributable to demographic expansion is a passive, market-level factor reflecting broader population trends in the metropolitan area.
>
> \(iii\) *Ancillary Diagnostic Service Demand Growth*: Increased utilization of echocardiography and other cardiac diagnostic procedures in the pediatric population, driven by evolving clinical guidelines published by the American Academy of Pediatrics and expanded screening recommendations, contributed to incremental diagnostic revenue. These utilization trends reflect broader changes in medical practice patterns rather than practice-specific clinical initiatives.

**[Active Components of Value and Growth]{.underline}**

Approximately **40%** of the Practice\'s revenue growth over the five-year review period is attributable to active factors involving the deliberate management decisions and clinical efforts of the physician-owners:

> \(i\) *Physician Recruitment* (approximately 25% of total revenue growth): The Practice recruited two new physician-partners and one nurse practitioner during the review period, expanding clinical capacity and the Practice\'s ability to serve a larger patient population. These recruitment decisions were made collectively by the existing partners, including Dr. Veselka-Thornton, and required active management involvement in needs assessment, candidate identification, credentialing, onboarding, and clinical integration.
>
> \(ii\) *Operational Improvements* (approximately 15% of total revenue growth): The Practice implemented a new electronic health records system (completed 2021), expanded its in-house ancillary diagnostic service offerings to include fetal echocardiography and ambulatory event monitoring, and renegotiated the terms of its hospital affiliation agreements with Lakeshore Children\'s Hospital. These initiatives required active planning, capital investment, and management oversight by the physician-owners.
>
> \(iii\) *Referral Network Development*: The physician-owners engaged in active cultivation of referral relationships with primary care pediatricians and family medicine practices in the region, including outreach programs, educational seminars, and collaborative care coordination initiatives.

**[Important Caveat]{.underline}**

The 60%/40% attribution analysis set forth above reflects the sources of *revenue growth* over the five-year review period. The attribution of *total practice value* --- as opposed to incremental growth --- between active and passive components is a more complex undertaking that would require additional analysis. The existing goodwill of the Practice, built over twelve years of continuous operation, includes both personal goodwill (attributable to individual physician reputations, clinical relationships, and professional standing) and enterprise goodwill (attributable to the Practice\'s brand, geographic location, operational systems, assembled workforce, and institutional infrastructure). A full allocation of total goodwill between personal and enterprise components was beyond the scope of this engagement but could be performed upon request in a separate, more comprehensive analysis.

**[Application Note]{.underline}**

If a distinction between active and passive appreciation were to be applied to Dr. Veselka-Thornton\'s 22% membership interest for purposes of classifying marital versus separate property in the context of the postnuptial agreement, the revenue growth attribution analysis presented herein suggests that a significant majority of any appreciation in the Practice\'s value during the review period --- approximately 60% --- would likely be attributable to passive, market-driven factors rather than to the active personal efforts of Dr. Veselka-Thornton individually. The remaining approximately 40% of growth reflects active management and operational decisions in which Dr. Veselka-Thornton participated as one of several physician-partners. The extent to which any particular classification standard would apply to the interest is a legal determination beyond the scope of this valuation engagement.

**[SECTION 10: ASSUMPTIONS AND LIMITING CONDITIONS]{.underline}**

This summary valuation report has been prepared subject to the following assumptions, limiting conditions, and qualifications:

1\. **Reliance on Information Provided.** The valuation analyses and conclusion set forth in this report are based upon financial information, operating data, and other material provided by Heartbeat Pediatric Partners LLC, its management, its accountants (Linden & Fosse CPA), and the legal counsel for Dr. Veselka-Thornton. Strand Valuation Services has not independently audited, verified, or compiled this information. While we have no reason to believe that the information provided is materially inaccurate or incomplete, we assume no responsibility for the accuracy or completeness of such information and have relied upon its substantial accuracy in reaching our conclusion of value.

2\. **Going Concern Assumption.** This valuation assumes that Heartbeat Pediatric Partners LLC will continue to operate as a going concern in its current form, and that its assets will continue to be employed in the production of income in the ordinary course of business. No consideration has been given to any potential liquidation, dissolution, or restructuring of the Practice.

3\. **Valuation Date Limitation.** The conclusion of value expressed herein is valid only as of the valuation date of November 30, 2024. The value of the subject interest may change with the passage of time, the occurrence of future events, or changes in economic, industry, or practice-specific conditions. Strand Valuation Services undertakes no obligation to update this report for events occurring after the valuation date.

4\. **Not a Recommendation.** This valuation does not constitute a recommendation to buy, sell, or otherwise transact with respect to any membership interest in Heartbeat Pediatric Partners LLC.

5\. **Restricted Use.** This report is intended solely for use by Diane Veselka-Thornton and her legal counsel, Kessler, Odom & Pratt LLP, in connection with the negotiation and execution of a postnuptial agreement between Diane Veselka-Thornton and Marcus Thornton. This report may not be used for any other purpose, and may not be distributed, reproduced, or provided to any third party, without the prior written consent of Strand Valuation Services.

6\. **Active/Passive Attribution Analysis.** The 60%/40% active/passive revenue growth attribution analysis set forth in Section 9 of this report is based upon management interviews, available financial data, industry benchmarking publications, and the professional judgment of Strand Valuation Services. This analysis involves the exercise of professional judgment, and different analysts applying different methodologies, data sources, or assumptions might reach different conclusions regarding the attribution of revenue growth between active and passive components. The attribution analysis is intended to provide a reasonable estimate and should not be construed as a definitive or precise allocation.

7\. **Tax Implications.** This valuation does not address the income tax, gift tax, estate tax, or other tax implications of any hypothetical transfer, division, or reclassification of the membership interest. The parties should consult with their respective tax advisors regarding any tax consequences.

8\. **Professional Standards.** This summary report has been prepared in accordance with the professional standards promulgated by the American Society of Appraisers (ASA) and in conformity with the Uniform Standards of Professional Appraisal Practice (USPAP). As a summary report, it does not include all of the detailed analyses, supporting data, and documentation that would be present in a comprehensive (detailed) valuation report. The complete working paper file, including supporting schedules, data sources, and analytical workpapers, is maintained by Strand Valuation Services and is available for review upon request.

9\. **Financial Disclosures.** Certain financial data relied upon in this engagement were coordinated through and provided by Linden & Fosse CPA, the certified public accounting firm retained to prepare financial disclosures for both parties in connection with the postnuptial agreement process. Strand Valuation Services has relied upon the representations of Linden & Fosse CPA as to the accuracy and completeness of such data.

**[SECTION 11: APPRAISER\'S QUALIFICATIONS]{.underline}**

**Robert M. Strand, ASA, CFA, CVA** Senior Managing Director, Strand Valuation Services

Mr. Strand is the founder and Senior Managing Director of Strand Valuation Services, a Chicago-based business valuation and financial advisory firm. He holds the designations of Accredited Senior Appraiser in Business Valuation (ASA) from the American Society of Appraisers, Chartered Financial Analyst (CFA) from the CFA Institute, and Certified Valuation Analyst (CVA) from the National Association of Certified Valuators and Analysts.

Mr. Strand has more than twenty years of professional experience in business valuation and financial consulting, with a particular specialization in the valuation of healthcare practices, physician-owned entities, and professional service firms. He has provided independent valuation opinions in connection with marital dissolution proceedings, postnuptial and prenuptial agreements, estate and gift tax planning, buy-sell agreements, partnership disputes, shareholder litigation, and regulatory compliance matters.

Mr. Strand holds a Bachelor of Science degree in Finance from the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign and a Master of Business Administration from the Kellogg School of Management at Northwestern University. He is a member of the American Society of Appraisers, the CFA Institute, and the CFA Society of Chicago. He is qualified to render business valuation opinions under the laws of the State of Illinois.

**[APPENDICES]{.underline}**

The following appendices are referenced in this summary valuation report. As this report is a summary report and not a comprehensive (detailed) report, the appendices listed below are described by reference only. The full supporting documentation, schedules, and analytical workpapers are maintained in the complete working paper file of Strand Valuation Services and are available upon request.

**Appendix A: Summary Financial Statements of Heartbeat Pediatric Partners LLC (2019--2024 YTD)** Summary income statements and balance sheets for the Practice for fiscal years 2019 through 2023, and for the ten-month period ended October 31, 2024. Financial data was sourced from the Practice\'s internally prepared financial statements, audited financial statements where available, and information provided by Linden & Fosse CPA.

**Appendix B: Comparable Transaction Data** Summary of the six comparable transactions involving physician-owned specialty practices in the Midwest region (2021--2024) used in the Market Approach analysis. Transaction details include acquirer, target, transaction date, transaction value, and derived valuation multiples. Source data was obtained from proprietary transaction databases, public filings, and industry publications.

**Appendix C: Discount Analysis Supporting Data** Summary of the restricted stock studies, pre-IPO discount studies, and minority interest transaction data used to support the selected Discount for Lack of Marketability (20%) and Discount for Lack of Control (10.7%). Includes citations to published studies and peer-reviewed articles relied upon in the analysis.

**Appendix D: Revenue Growth Attribution Methodology and Supporting Calculations** Detailed description of the methodology used to decompose the Practice\'s revenue growth between active and passive components, including data sources, analytical steps, assumptions, and supporting calculations. Includes reference to CMS rate tables, Census Bureau demographic data, MGMA benchmarking surveys, and management interview notes.

**END OF SUMMARY VALUATION REPORT**

"""Generate a .docx from markdown via Pandoc with an optional reference template.

Usage:
    python generate_from_md.py input.md output.docx [template.docx]

Pandoc applies the template's paragraph and run styles to your markdown.
Best for reports/memos/letters where the template carries firm styling.

Requires pandoc on PATH.
"""
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path


def generate(md_path: Path, output_path: Path, template_path: Path | None = None):
    output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    cmd = ["pandoc", str(md_path), "-o", str(output_path)]
    if template_path is not None:
        cmd.append(f"--reference-doc={template_path}")
    result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True)
    if result.returncode != 0:
        print(f"pandoc failed: {result.stderr}", file=sys.stderr)

    print(f"OK: wrote {output_path}")

result:
"""Generate a .docx from markdown via Pandoc with an optional reference template.

Usage:
    python generate_from_md.py input.md output.docx [template.docx]

Pandoc applies the template's paragraph and run styles to your markdown.
Best for reports/memos/letters where the template carries firm styling.

Requires pandoc on PATH.
"""
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path


def generate(md_path: Path, output_path: Path, template_path: Path | None = None):
    output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    cmd = ["pandoc", str(md_path), "-o", str(output_path)]
    if template_path is not None:
        cmd.append(f"--reference-doc={template_path}")
    result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True)
    if result.returncode != 0:
        print(f"pandoc failed: {result.stderr}", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)
    print(f"OK: wrote {output_path}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    if len(sys.argv) not in (3, 4):
        print("Usage: generate_from_md.py <input.md> <output.docx> [template.docx]", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(2)
    template = Path(sys.argv[3]) if len(sys.argv) == 4 else None
    generate(Path(sys.argv[1]), Path(sys.argv[2]), template)

text: I have all the information I need. Let me build a comprehensive issue inventory and then draft the memo. First, let me record my analysis notes to ensure I capture every distinct issue.

tool_calls.name: bash
tool_calls.arguments:
{"command": "cat > /workspace/analysis-notes.md << 'NOTES'
# ISSUE INVENTORY (working notes — not deliverable)

## Parties / counsel
- Wife: Diane Veselka-Thornton (pediatric cardiologist, Lakeshore Children's Hospital). OUR CLIENT.
- Husband: Marcus Thornton (SVP BD, Grayfield Industries). Opposing.
- Wife's counsel: Catherine Odom, Kessler, Odom & Pratt LLP (us).
- Husband's counsel: Thomas Baxter, Hargrove Baxter LLP (redline author).
- Original draft = OUR draft (transmitted Dec 16). Redline = Baxter's markup (Jan 6). Four-way conf Jan 27.

## FACTUAL DISCREPANCIES (cross-document) — VERIFY
1. Children birth dates: Original (Elena Oct 3 2012; Julian Mar 22 2016) vs Redline (Elena Aug 22 2012; Julian Nov 9 2015) vs Intake (Elena 12, Julian 9). Redline Julian matches age 9; original Julian (Mar 2016) = age 8, wrong. Elena exact date unverified. BOTH place Elena birth BEFORE June 14 2013 marriage -> "born of the marriage" inaccurate. Verify w/ birth certs.
2. Mother death date: Original Aug 12 2024 vs Redline Sept 3 2024 vs Intake/xlsx "October 2024". THREE different. Verify w/ death cert.
3. Mother name: Original "Constane" (misspelled) vs Redline "Constance (née Albrecht)" vs Intake "Constance". Redline corrected; maiden name unverified.
4. Inheritance brokerage custodian: Original "Hartleigh Investments" vs Redline "Tidewater Securities (acct -7724)" vs xlsx "Northbridge Wealth Advisors (acct **8837)". THREE different. Verify.
5. Husband 401(k) custodian: Redline "Hartleigh Investments" vs xlsx "Northbridge Wealth Advisors (acct **5518)". Different. Verify.
6. Joint Investment Acct number: Redline "-3961" vs xlsx "**3291". Different. Verify.
7. Wife 403(b) custodian: Redline "TIAA-CREF" vs xlsx "Northbridge Wealth Advisors (acct **7842)". Different. Verify.
8. Joint savings/checking: Redline "First Saxonbrook Bank and Hollcroft Fiduciary" vs xlsx "First Saxonbrook Bank" only. Redline adds Hollcroft. Verify.
9. Door County appraiser: Redline "Midwest Appraisal Associates (Oct 15 2024)" vs xlsx "Pendleton Appraisal Group". Different. Verify.
10. Wife retirement split: Redline ($1.44M 403b + $0.9M pension) vs xlsx ($1.415M + $0.925M). Both sum $2.34M. Verify.
11. Wife individual CC: Redline "$4,200" vs xlsx "$3,200 (Valemont Visa **4412)". Different. Verify.
12. Identified Debt characterization: Redline "multiple revolving credit accounts / various issuers" vs xlsx "Hollcroft One Mastercard **6673, $68,000" (single) vs Intake "a hidden credit card" (singular). Redline fragments. Verify.
13. Wife income/distributions: Redline Schedule A only $487K W-2, distributions "included in W-2/K-1". Valuation report: ~$145K/yr distributions SEPARATE. Wife gross ~$632K. Disclosure understates. Verify/correct.
14. Husband disclosed CC ($4,800 Amex **2201 per xlsx): omitted from redline Schedule B. Minor.
15. Automobiles (Wife BMW $52K; Husband Mercedes $58K per xlsx): unaddressed in both agreements. $110K marital assets. Minor.
16. Wife hospital address: Redline adds "680 N Lake Shore Dr, Chicago 60611". Unverified. Minor.
17. Wife practice start: Redline "since 2011" vs Intake "over 15 yrs" (~2009) vs Valuation "Practice founded ~2012". Minor.

## MAJOR ADVERSE CHANGES (red flags)
18. TC-22/MC-05: DELETE Wife $1.2M premarital savings reimbursement (Sec 5.2). Client #1 non-negotiable. Wife loses ~$600K. Comment falsely "duplicative."
19. TC-42: Voidability -> INTENTIONAL concealment >$100K, CLEAR & CONVINCING, 2-YR limit. Contradicts client Obj 2 + intake warning. $100K just above $68K prior debt.
20. Delete ongoing disclosure duty (orig Sec 3.4). Contradicts "ongoing annual disclosure" desire.
21. Delete future-debt disclosure duty (orig Sec 11.3).
22. TC-46: NEW unilateral modification by notice, effective 90 days unless objection. NO comment. Contradicts Obj 5(f). Dangerous embedded term.
23. TC-19/MC-04: Transmutation REVERSED -> SP used for joint benefit NOT commingled; reimbursement claim only; stays SP. Reverses orig "gift to marriage." Primarily protects Husband $3.8M inheritance. Hurts Wife (could lose $300K+ on mortgage paydown).
24. TC-18/MC-03: Commingling softened "may" vs orig "shall be treated as Marital."
25. TC-27/MC-07: RSU coverture fraction. Hire-date (Mar 15 2018) start = MORE Wife-favorable than grant-date (higher fraction). Modest reduction vs orig 50/50-of-all for unvested-at-dissolution tranches (~$18-28K if dissolution pre-2027; $0 if after). Orig 50/50 most Wife-favorable & defensible (granted+vesting during marriage). Comment "more equitable" misleading.
26. TC-08: Marital Property narrowed to "from Earned Income or joint efforts." Excludes bonuses/equity/investment/distributions. Ambiguity; shrinks marital estate; hurts Wife.
27. TC-10/MC-02: NEW "Earned Income" def excluding bonuses/commissions/equity/RSUs/options/variable comp. Narrows base. Misleading "for clarity."
28. Delete "Gross Income" def (orig 2.1(d)). Replaced by narrow Earned Income.
29. TC-44/MC-11: Fees -> mutual waiver vs orig prevailing-party. Contradicts Obj 5(d). Removes deterrent.
30. TC-47/MC-12: Venue -> DuPage vs orig Cook. Contradicts Obj 5(e). Residence Winnetka (Cook); inconvenient/unenforceable for dissolution.
31. Sec 15.5: Sunset 15yr (~2040) -> 7yr (~2032). Contradicts Obj 3 (cover to Julian 18 ~2033/34). Expires before Julian majority.
32. TC-40/MC-10: Identified Debt indemnity -> claims >$5K individually (de minimis). Contradicts Obj 2(d). + fragmentation (#12) could gut indemnity.
33. TC-06/TC-41: Disclosure "full, fair, complete" -> "substantially complete." Weakens warranty.
34. TC-15: "satisfied with sufficiency" -> "generally satisfied." Weakens.

## FAVORABLE TO WIFE (accept w/ caveats)
35. TC-36/MC-08: Maintenance cap 5yr -> 3yr. Benefits Wife (payor). Enforceability ok.
36. TC-37/MC-09: NEW auto waiver if recipient gross income >$200K. Eliminates Husband maint ($305K>$200K). Benefits Wife. Likely enforceable (he has $3.8M inheritance + $305K). Comment "benefits both equally" misleading. Fixed $200K favors Wife over time (inflation). Confirm w/ client.
37. TC-25/MC-06: Practice appreciation active/passive. Only active marital. Benefits Wife (val report ~60% passive). Supported by Strand Sec 9. Adds valuation complexity (client wanted to avoid). Accept w/ methodology safeguards.
38. Delete Practice Interest transfer restriction (orig Sec 6.3). Benefits Wife autonomy. Operating Ag already restricts. Accept.
39. 24-mo Identified Debt satisfaction + quarterly stmts (new). Concrete accountability. Accept (w/ removing de minimis).
40. TC-09(v): SP appreciation protected. Supports Wife passive practice appreciation. Accept.

## BENIGN/STANDARD (accept)
41. Severability (Sec 15.3). Accept.
42. Notarization + witness blocks. Accept.
43. TC-02: "Constane"->"Constance". Accept (verify death date separately).
44. TC-04/MC-01: marriage-commitment recital. Benign. Accept.
45. TC-01: draft markings. Accept.
46. TC-12: Effective Date w/ witnesses/notary. Accept.
47. TC-13: "Net Worth" def. Benign. Accept.
48. TC-07/TC-11: "Agreement"/"Dissolution" reworded. Similar. Accept.
49. TC-14: "Children" defined term. Accept (verify dates).
50. Reorg to Roman articles / recitals in Art I. Accept but check dropped provisions (see E).

## OMISSIONS / DROPPED IN REORG (restore)
51. "No Pending Proceedings" rep (orig 15.10). Omitted. Restore.
52. Fiduciary duty acknowledgment (orig 15.9). Omitted. Restore (given Husband non-disclosure).
53. Maintenance termination events (orig 10.4: death/remarriage/cohabitation/cap). Omitted. Restore cohabitation (protects Wife payor).
54. Ongoing disclosure duty (orig 3.4). Restore. (=#20)
55. Future-debt disclosure duty (orig 11.3). Restore. (=#21)
56. Mortgage obligations during marriage / consent to encumbrance (orig 9.4). Restore.
57. Valuation of marital property general (orig 5.3). Minor restore.
58. RSU division mechanisms (orig 7.2: direct transfer/constructive trust/cash). Restore specificity.
59. Marital Residence during-marriage occupancy rights (orig 9.4). Minor.

## MARGIN COMMENT ASSESSMENT
- MC-05 (reimb "duplicative"): FALSE.
- MC-02 (Earned Inc "for clarity"): MISLEADING.
- MC-04 (transmutation "protects both"): MISLEADING (primarily Husband).
- MC-07 (coverture "more equitable"): MISLEADING (reduces Wife vs orig; though hire-date is less adverse).
- MC-09 (waiver "benefits both equally"): MISLEADING (eliminates Husband maint).
- MC-10 (de minimis "standard commercial"): MISLEADING (family indemnity loophole).
- MC-11 (fee waiver "balanced"): MISLEADING (removes deterrent).
- MC-12 (DuPage "scheduling"): MISLEADING (residence Cook).
- MC-03 (commingling "aligns tracing"): PARTIAL (softens rule).
- MC-06 (active/passive "protects Wife"): ACCURATE.
- MC-08 (3yr "5 excessive"): debatable; benefits Wife.
- MC-01: benign.
- TC-46 & TC-42 have NO comments -> adverse terms slipped in. Matches client warning re "hidden/embedded terms."

## EMAIL DISCONNECT
- Baxter email frames as "modest clarifications"/"balanced"/"nothing far from where we started." Only flags severability, notarization, spelling, marriage-commitment, sunset, DuPage. BURIES: reimbursement deletion, coverture, transmutation reversal, voidability weakening, unilateral mod, fee waiver, Earned Income narrowing. Framing inconsistent w/ substance.

## FINANCIAL IMPACT (on Wife if accepted as-is)
1. Reimb deletion: -$600,000 (certain). [JIA $1.87M: orig Wife $1.535M vs redline $0.935M]
2. Transmutation reversal: speculative, potentially -$300K+ (mortgage paydown etc).
3. RSU coverture: -$0 to ~$28K (contingent on dissolution pre-Jan 2027).
4. Maintenance waiver/cap: SAVES (eliminates Husband maint). Contingent/enforceability. Favorable.
5. Active/passive practice: GAINS (Wife keeps ~80% of appreciation vs ~50%). Favorable.
6. Earned Income narrowing: ambiguous/risky; could shrink marital estate; could RAISE maint if waiver struck (excl Husband bonus lowers his income -> higher cap room). Illustrative: Earned-Income maint ~$65.8K/yr vs Gross-Income (Wife $487K) ~$11.8K/yr. Counterintuitive.
7. Voidability weakening: risk = entire agreement balance if Husband hides assets.
8. Fee waiver: risk = enforcement fees.
9. De minimis debt: risk up to $68K + creditor exposure.
10. Sunset 7yr: loss of protections after ~2032.
11. Unilateral mod: could undermine any provision.

NET: redline shifts substantial value to Husband (>=$600K certain + contingent) for speculative Wife savings. Net adverse.

## NEGOTIATION TIERS
T1 (must restore — client core): reimb right (#18); voidability standard + ongoing/future disclosure (#19,20,21); unilateral mod (#22); transmutation (#23); de minimis debt + correct fragmentation (#32,#12); fees prevailing-party (#29); venue Cook (#30); sunset 15yr (#31); fiduciary/no-pending reps (#51,52); cohabitation termination (#53).
T2 (strong pushback): commingling soften (#24); Marital Property/Earned Income narrowing (#26,27,28); disclosure "substantially" (#33,34); RSU keep 50/50 or accept coverture w/ hire-date (#25); mortgage consent (#56).
T3 (accept favorable, lock in): maint 3yr cap (#35); maint $200K waiver (#36); active/passive practice (#37); delete transfer restriction (#38); 24-mo debt deadline (#39); SP appreciation (#40). Confirm maint w/ client.
T4 (accept benign): #41-50.
VERIFY: all #1-17 before execution.
NOTES
echo "Notes saved.""}

32. TC-40/MC-10: Identified Debt indemnity -> claims >$5K individually (de minimis). Contradicts Obj 2(d). + fragmentation (#12) could gut indemnity.
33. TC-06/TC-41: Disclosure "full, fair, complete" -> "substantially complete." Weakens warranty.
34. TC-15: "satisfied with sufficiency" -> "generally satisfied." Weakens.

## FAVORABLE TO WIFE (accept w/ caveats)
35. TC-36/MC-08: Maintenance cap 5yr -> 3yr. Benefits Wife (payor). Enforceability ok.
36. TC-37/MC-09: NEW auto waiver if recipient gross income >$200K. Eliminates Husband maint ($305K>$200K). Benefits Wife. Likely enforceable (he has $3.8M inheritance + $305K). Comment "benefits both equally" misleading. Fixed $200K favors Wife over time (inflation). Confirm w/ client.
37. TC-25/MC-06: Practice appreciation active/passive. Only active marital. Benefits Wife (val report ~60% passive). Supported by Strand Sec 9. Adds valuation complexity (client wanted to avoid). Accept w/ methodology safeguards.
38. Delete Practice Interest transfer restriction (orig Sec 6.3). Benefits Wife autonomy. Operating Ag already restricts. Accept.
39. 24-mo Identified Debt satisfaction + quarterly stmts (new). Concrete accountability. Accept (w/ removing de minimis).
40. TC-09(v): SP appreciation protected. Supports Wife passive practice appreciation. Accept.

## BENIGN/STANDARD (accept)
41. Severability (Sec 15.3). Accept.
42. Notarization + witness blocks. Accept.
43. TC-02: "Constane"->"Constance". Accept (verify death date separately).
44. TC-04/MC-01: marriage-commitment recital. Benign. Accept.
45. TC-01: draft markings. Accept.
46. TC-12: Effective Date w/ witnesses/notary. Accept.
47. TC-13: "Net Worth" def. Benign. Accept.
48. TC-07/TC-11: "Agreement"/"Dissolution" reworded. Similar. Accept.
49. TC-14: "Children" defined term. Accept (verify dates).
50. Reorg to Roman articles / recitals in Art I. Accept but check dropped provisions (see E).

## OMISSIONS / DROPPED IN REORG (restore)
51. "No Pending Proceedings" rep (orig 15.10). Omitted. Restore.
52. Fiduciary duty acknowledgment (orig 15.9). Omitted. Restore (given Husband non-disclosure).
53. Maintenance termination events (orig 10.4: death/remarriage/cohabitation/cap). Omitted. Restore cohabitation (protects Wife payor).
54. Ongoing disclosure duty (orig 3.4). Restore. (=#20)
55. Future-debt disclosure duty (orig 11.3). Restore. (=#21)
56. Mortgage obligations during marriage / consent to encumbrance (orig 9.4). Restore.
57. Valuation of marital property general (orig 5.3). Minor restore.
58. RSU division mechanisms (orig 7.2: direct transfer/constructive trust/cash). Restore specificity.
59. Marital Residence during-marriage occupancy rights (orig 9.4). Minor.

## MARGIN COMMENT ASSESSMENT
- MC-05 (reimb "duplicative"): FALSE.
- MC-02 (Earned Inc "for clarity"): MISLEADING.
- MC-04 (transmutation "protects both"): MISLEADING (primarily Husband).
- MC-07 (coverture "more equitable"): MISLEADING (reduces Wife vs orig; though hire-date is less adverse).
- MC-09 (waiver "benefits both equally"): MISLEADING (eliminates Husband maint).
- MC-10 (de minimis "standard commercial"): MISLEADING (family indemnity loophole).
- MC-11 (fee waiver "balanced"): MISLEADING (removes deterrent).
- MC-12 (DuPage "scheduling"): MISLEADING (residence Cook).
- MC-03 (commingling "aligns tracing"): PARTIAL (softens rule).
- MC-06 (active/passive "protects Wife"): ACCURATE.
- MC-08 (3yr "5 excessive"): debatable; benefits Wife.
- MC-01: benign.
- TC-46 & TC-42 have NO comments -> adverse terms slipped in. Matches client warning re "hidden/embedded terms."

## EMAIL DISCONNECT
- Baxter email frames as "modest clarifications"/"balanced"/"nothing far from where we started." Only flags severability, notarization, spelling, marriage-commitment, sunset, DuPage. BURIES: reimbursement deletion, coverture, transmutation reversal, voidability weakening, unilateral mod, fee waiver, Earned Income narrowing. Framing inconsistent w/ substance.

## FINANCIAL IMPACT (on Wife if accepted as-is)
1. Reimb deletion: -$600,000 (certain). [JIA $1.87M: orig Wife $1.535M vs redline $0.935M]
2. Transmutation reversal: speculative, potentially -$300K+ (mortgage paydown etc).
3. RSU coverture: -$0 to ~$28K (contingent on dissolution pre-Jan 2027).
4. Maintenance waiver/cap: SAVES (eliminates Husband maint). Contingent/enforceability. Favorable.
5. Active/passive practice: GAINS (Wife keeps ~80% of appreciation vs ~50%). Favorable.
6. Earned Income narrowing: ambiguous/risky; could shrink marital estate; could RAISE maint if waiver struck (excl Husband bonus lowers his income -> higher cap room). Illustrative: Earned-Income maint ~$65.8K/yr vs Gross-Income (Wife $487K) ~$11.8K/yr. Counterintuitive.
7. Voidability weakening: risk = entire agreement balance if Husband hides assets.
8. Fee waiver: risk = enforcement fees.
9. De minimis debt: risk up to $68K + creditor exposure.
10. Sunset 7yr: loss of protections after ~2032.
11. Unilateral mod: could undermine any provision.

NET: redline shifts substantial value to Husband (>=$600K certain + contingent) for speculative Wife savings. Net adverse.

## NEGOTIATION TIERS
T1 (must restore — client core): reimb right (#18); voidability standard + ongoing/future disclosure (#19,20,21); unilateral mod (#22); transmutation (#23); de minimis debt + correct fragmentation (#32,#12); fees prevailing-party (#29); venue Cook (#30); sunset 15yr (#31); fiduciary/no-pending reps (#51,52); cohabitation termination (#53).
T2 (strong pushback): commingling soften (#24); Marital Property/Earned Income narrowing (#26,27,28); disclosure "substantially" (#33,34); RSU keep 50/50 or accept coverture w/ hire-date (#25); mortgage consent (#56).
T3 (accept favorable, lock in): maint 3yr cap (#35); maint $200K waiver (#36); active/passive practice (#37); delete transfer restriction (#38); 24-mo debt deadline (#39); SP appreciation (#40). Confirm maint w/ client.
T4 (accept benign): #41-50.
VERIFY: all #1-17 before execution.
NOTES
